steps.m: to witness or observe something directly

Added ُ to show this word is doing the action in the sentence
Added kasra (ِ) under إ to show correct pronunciation. This is a particle that excludes what comes after it from what came before it.

steps.m: their selves (referring to the husbands mentioned in أَزْوٰجَهُمْ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show these are their own selves. This refers back to the husbands mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: to witness or testify to something you saw

steps.n: Added basic vowels to connect the root letters and make it a past tense verb
Added alif after second letter to make it a noun showing the action itself
Added ة to show this is one specific instance of giving testimony
Added فَ at beginning to show this follows from what was mentioned before
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence. The alif is written as ٰ which is just a writing variation.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic noun
Added kasra to show this word is being possessed by something
Added هِمْ to show this belongs to them or is one from their group

steps.m: to be or make into four parts

steps.n: Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Making the word into a noun by changing the middle vowel
Changed from 'fourth part' to the number 'four' by adding أ at start and damma at end to show it's counting testimonies
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

Added feminine plural suffix ات to create regular feminine plural form
The alif is written as a small alif (dagger alif) above the letters in this style of writing
Added tanwin kasra to show this is being counted as part of أَرْبَعُ (four) testimonies
This is the unique proper name of God in Islam. The word comes from the root meaning 'divine' but has special spelling and meaning as God's name.

steps.m: by Allah (object of swearing in أَرْبَعُ شَهٰدٰتٍۭ)
steps.n: Added بِ at the beginning to show swearing by something, and ِ at the end because it's being acted upon. In this ayah, it's part of taking four testimonies by swearing by Allah.

Adds 'he' to show who is being emphasized. This connects to the next phrase 'among the truthful ones'.
The small و mark (ۥ) is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Added fatha at the end to help connect it with the next word
Added لَ at beginning to add emphasis. This emphasizes the statement that follows in the oath, connecting to إِنَّهُۥ (indeed he) in the ayah.

Added alif after first letter and kasra after second letter to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people

Added feminine marker to make word refer to feminine things
Added definite article to specify this particular fifth thing

Added damma to show this is the subject of the sentence

Changed from a verb (he cursed) to a noun (cursing) by making the middle letter silent

Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added kasra at the end to show possession/belonging
Added vowel marks to the basic preposition على which means 'upon'

steps.m: he was (in the condition: if he was one of the liars)

This is the basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to
Added fatha at the end to connect it to the next word الْكٰذِبِينَ (the liars). This shows we're talking about being from a specific group.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action

steps.m: people who tell untrue things (after مِنَ)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to show we're talking about many people. The ِ vowel shows it comes after the word مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah.

steps.m: the people who tell untrue things (after مِنَ)
steps.n: Added ال at the beginning to point to specific people who tell untrue things
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make past tense verb
Added ي with fatha at start and changed last vowel to damma to show present tense
Added و with fatha at start to connect this action with what came before it
Added alif and small و at end to show multiple people doing the action
This is a preposition that shows something moving away from something else
The suffix ها refers to a feminine singular third person pronoun (her/it)
Added vowels to connect the letters together and show this happened in the past

Adding ال makes it about a specific punishment
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being acted upon

Added vowels to connect the root letters and make a past tense verb

Added short vowels (fatha) to make the basic word for 'four'
Added أَ at beginning and changed middle vowel to sukun to form the number pattern. This word is the object of تَشْهَدَ (she testifies).
Added fatha at end to show this is being acted upon by the verb تَشْهَدَ (she testifies)
Basic past tense verb meaning 'to witness'
Changes from verb (witnessed) to noun (testimony) by adding alif
Added ة to specify one instance of testimony
Added ات to make it plural (follows the number أربع/four in the ayah)
Changed regular alifs to small alifs and added tanwin kasra (ٍ) at the end to show it's part of a possession/ownership phrase
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation of Allah's name
Added a small line under the last letter to show it belongs to/is connected to another word
Added بِ at beginning to mean 'by/with' and ٱ (alif with waṣla) as a pronunciation helper
This is a particle that makes what comes after it stronger or more certain
Added هُ which is a pronoun meaning 'he/it' (referring to the husband in this ayah who is being accused of lying)
Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
Added fatha at the end to help connect with the next word (الكاذبين)
Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, showing strong certainty. This particle is often used in oaths or strong statements.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Added alif after first letter and kasra under second letter to show who does the action
Added ين at the end to make it plural. The kasra before ين shows it follows مِنَ (from/of) in the ayah
Added ال at beginning to make it specific. The alif has a special mark (ٰ) to show it's pronounced long
Added vowels to make the basic number 'five' from the root letters
Changes 'five' into 'fifth' by adding alif after first letter
Makes the number feminine by adding ta marbuta
Shows this word is being acted upon in the sentence
Makes it refer to a specific fifth thing

steps.m: and the fifth (feminine, object) (testimony in the case of لِعَان)
steps.n: The 'wa' (وَ) was added at the start to mean 'and', connecting this fifth testimony to the previous four testimonies
Starting with the basic connecting particle that joins clauses together
Adding shadda makes it stronger and more emphatic. In this ayah, it connects to the word غَضَبَ (anger) to emphasize what comes after it.

steps.m: to be angry, to feel wrath

steps.n: Added fathas to make the root letters into a noun meaning 'wrath'. When used with اللَّهِ (Allah) in this ayah, it means 'Allah's wrath'.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
The word for 'god' (ilah) becomes 'Allah' - the special name for the one true God. The small i-vowel at the end (kasra) connects it to the next word.
This is a preposition showing something is on top of or directed towards something else
The ى changes to ي before adding pronouns
Added ها which shows this applies to a feminine thing or person. In this ayah, it shows who Allah's anger is directed upon.

steps.m: if (connecting إِن with كَانَ to mean 'if he was')
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it links with كَانَ (he was) to express 'if he was'. It's a basic word that doesn't come from a root.

steps.m: to exist, to be, to happen

steps.n: Added fatha vowels to make it past tense. The middle و will change in the next step because it's a weak letter.
The weak letter و changes to ا, which is a common change in hollow verbs
This is a preposition showing something comes from somewhere or belongs to something
Added fatha at the end to connect it with the next word 'the truthful ones' (ٱلصَّادِقِينَ). This combination means 'from the'.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changes from 'spoke truthfully' (a verb) to 'truthful person' (describes someone). Like changing 'ran' to 'runner'
Changed from singular 'truthful person' to plural 'truthful people' by adding ين at the end

This is a compound particle made up of لو (if) and لا (not)

steps.m: and if not for (introduces فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ - the favor of Allah)

steps.m: to give extra or do more than required

steps.n: Root letters put together with vowels to make a noun meaning 'favor/blessing' instead of a verb. Shows what Allah gives to people.
Added ُ to show this is the subject of لَوْلَا (if not for) in this ayah
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
The word for 'god' (ilah) becomes 'Allah' - the special name for the one true God. The small i-vowel at the end (kasra) connects it to the next word.

The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns - this is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning

steps.n: Added كُمْ which means 'you all' when speaking to a group. In this ayah, it shows who Allah's favor is upon.

Added ُ to show this word is connected to فَضْلُ (favor) in the sentence

one who repeatedly accepts repentance (refers to Allah)

Added هُم at the end to show this means 'them' (referring to the group mentioned in the ayah)

steps.m: its major part (object of تَوَلَّىٰ/took charge of)
steps.n: Added هُ to show it belongs to something and َ to show it's being acted upon. This word is what's being 'taken charge of' in the verse.

steps.m: from them (referring to the group who spread the false accusation)

Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose

Added the small و mark (ۥ) which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning

Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that describes the action. This word is in nominative case and is described by the adjective عَظِيمٌ (great/severe) which follows it.
Adding tanween makes the noun indefinite. This word is in nominative case and is described by the adjective عَظِيمٌ (great/severe) which follows it.
Adding ي between ظ and م makes this describe something as having a strong quality. Here it's describing how severe the punishment is.
The ٌ shows this is describing an undefined punishment - meaning any great punishment, not a specific one
This is a compound word made from لو (if) and لا (not) that together create a new meaning. The sukun on و shows where the first part ends.

steps.m: why did they not (questioning the believers' response)
steps.n: The elongated alif (آ) at the end adds further emphasis and extends the sound, showing this is an important rhetorical question about the believers' reaction

steps.m: when (referring to سَمِعْتُمُوهُ - you heard it)

Added وهُ to show the object (it) that was heard

Adding vowels makes this a past tense verb about feeling safe
Changes from an action to someone who does that action regularly
Changes from one believer to many believers by adding ون at the end
Shows we're talking about specific believers and adds a mark to show this word is part of a longer sentence

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, believe in

steps.n: Changes the basic meaning into someone who does the action. The مُ at the start and the ِ in the middle work together to show who does something.
Adding ة at the end makes the word feminine, changing it from describing a male believer to a female believer

Adds وَ (and) to connect this word with the previous word المؤمنون (believing men)
Adds definite article to make the noun definite
Forms the plural pattern for 'self/soul'. This pattern (أَفْعُل) is commonly used for words that refer to small groups (3-10) of something.
Adds possession to show these are their own selves. In this ayah, it's emphasizing that the believers should have thought better about their fellow believers.

steps.n: The بِ here means 'about/concerning' rather than literally 'with'. This word is connected to ظَنَّ (thought) earlier in the ayah, showing what the believers should have thought about.

Added tanween to show this word is the object of ظَنَّ (they assumed/thought). The believers should have thought good about themselves instead of believing the false accusations.

steps.m: and they said (subject of هٰذَآ إِفْكٌ مُّبِينٌ)

The final alif is elongated (آ instead of ا) for emphasis in Quranic writing. This is a common spelling variation that doesn't change the core meaning.
Changed the first vowel to kasra and added sukun on middle letter to make it a noun meaning the result of lying
Added tanwin damma to show this is the subject being referred to by هٰذَآ (this) in the ayah
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
The مُ prefix and ِ vowel pattern creates an active participle - something or someone that does the action of making things clear
The shadda (doubled letter) is added because the word comes after a noon saakin, making the 'meem' doubled for easier pronunciation
The tanween shows this is describing the word إِفْكٌ (slander) in this ayah, indicating it was a clear/manifest lie
This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical situations
Added لا (not) to create a compound particle meaning 'if not for'
Added emphasis by doubling the first letter with a shadda
Added vowels and changed ي to ا because it's a special type of verb. The middle letter changes to make it easier to say.
Added و with damma (ُ) before it to show many people did the action. This word is connected to لَّوْلَا (why not) in the verse.

steps.m: upon (ready to connect to a pronoun)
steps.n: Changed the ending ى to يْ to prepare for connecting with the pronoun. This is just a spelling change to make the connection possible.

steps.m: upon him (referring to the evidence they should have brought)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end which means 'him'. In this ayah, it refers to the evidence or proof that the accusers should have brought.
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changed from verb to noun by making the middle letter silent
Makes it into the common word for 'four' by adding letters at both ends
Added بِ meaning 'with' and ِ showing it connects to the word شُهَدَآءَ (witnesses) after it

steps.m: to witness or testify to something you've seen

Added ي to show this describes someone who does the action of witnessing
Changed to plural form using the pattern for groups of people

steps.m: witnesses (as the object being brought forward)
steps.n: Added the fatha at the end to show this is the object being brought forward in the phrase 'bring forth witnesses'
This is a time particle that refers to something that happened in the past. In this ayah, it's used to point to when they didn't bring witnesses.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from the previous statement about bringing witnesses. It connects the question 'why didn't they' with what happens next.
This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of the verb after it (يَأْتُوا۟) to its opposite. It also changes the verb's time to the past.
Added sukun (ْ) at the end to show where the word stops
Past tense verb formed from root letters. The ى at the end shows it's past tense 'he came'
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense. The ى changes to ي in present tense.
Changed the ending to وا to show multiple people doing the action. The ي at the end is dropped.

steps.m: to witness or testify to something you saw

steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters to show someone who does the action
Changed the word to show many witnesses instead of just one
Added ال to show we're talking about specific witnesses
Added بِ at the beginning to show 'with' and ِ at the end because it follows the word بِ
This is the basic form of the word that points to things or people far away
Added the hamza with kasra to connect to the next part
Added ك at the end which completes the demonstrative pronoun
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before. In this ayah, it connects to the previous statement about not bringing witnesses.
This word is used to show location or possession, like saying something is 'at' or 'with' someone
Adding the kasra vowel mark to make it into a preposition showing location or possession
Adding the fatha at the end because this word is connected to Allah (اللَّهِ) in the ayah, showing something is 'with/near Allah'
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it specific, the doubled ل shows emphasis, and the ِ shows it belongs to or connects with what comes after it.
This is the basic pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' for a group of males or mixed group
The sukun (ْ) changes to damma (ُ) because this word connects to الْكٰذِبُونَ (the liars). This shows that 'they' are the ones being called liars.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to active participle form to show someone who lies
Added the definite article to specify these particular liars
Added the masculine plural ending ون to show multiple liars. This word describes هم (they) in the ayah.
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation. This is a fixed particle meaning 'if not for' that introduces a condition.
Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before it

steps.m: to give extra or more than needed

steps.n: Changes from verb to noun meaning 'extra/more' to 'favor/grace'
Added ُ to show this is the subject after لَوْلَا (if not for). The full phrase لَوْلَا فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ means 'if not for Allah's favor'.
Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Added ال and a shadda to make it specifically refer to Allah, the One True God. The first letter changes to match the ل sound.
Added kasra at the end to show possession - this word owns/possesses فَضْلُ (favor/grace) in this ayah
The ى at the end changes to ي with sukun when adding a suffix. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added كُمْ at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to. This is like changing 'upon' to 'upon you all' in English.
Adding vowels to create the basic noun meaning 'mercy'
The ة makes it refer to a specific instance of mercy
The ة changes to ت and gets a ُ on top to show this mercy is doing something (like 'mercy came')
The هُ shows this belongs to Allah
Added وَ to connect this word to what came before it
This is a basic preposition that shows location or containment. In this ayah, it connects the word عَذَابٌ (punishment) with مَآ (what), showing the punishment is in/regarding what was done.
Adding vowel marks to make basic past tense verb pattern
Added special pattern that makes it mean 'closer/nearer' and then became used specifically for this world because it's closer to us than the next life
Added ال to make it specific - this refers to our current world/life as opposed to the afterlife (الآخرة) mentioned right after it in the ayah

steps.m: and the one that comes after, be last
steps.n: Added ال to make it specific
Forms active participle meaning 'last/final' by adding long alif and kasra. This describes something that comes after everything else.
Adding ة makes it refer specifically to the final life (afterlife) that comes after this worldly life
Adding the kasra shows this word is connected to الدنيا (this world) in the phrase 'this world and the afterlife'

steps.m: to touch or feel something physically

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels and doubled the middle letter (shown by ّ) because two identical letters come together
Adding لَ at start shows emphasis/certainty of the action

steps.m: would surely touch/affect you all (consequence in وَلَوْلَا - if not for... statement)
steps.n: Added كُمْ at the end to show this action would happen to multiple people being addressed. This word is part of a conditional statement showing what would have happened if not for Allah's favor and mercy.

steps.m: in (connects ٱلدُّنْيَا to فَضْلُ and رَحْمَتُهُ)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows location or being inside something. Here it connects 'the worldly life' to Allah's 'favor and mercy' mentioned before it.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects two parts of a sentence. Here it connects to the verb أَفَضْتُمْ (you plunged into) to mean 'what you plunged into'.
The alif is written in its lengthened form (آ) instead of regular alif (ا). This is just a spelling variation that doesn't change the meaning or pronunciation.

steps.m: to flow or spread out abundantly

steps.n: Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic past tense verb. The middle letter ي becomes ا in past tense.
Added أَ at beginning to show causing something to happen
Added تُم at end to show 'you all' did the action
Word already has Form IV pattern with causative meaning
Combination of preposition في with attached pronoun ه meaning 'it'
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun that names the punishment itself. This word works with عَظِيمٌ (great/severe) to tell us about a great punishment.
Adding the double-damma (tanween) makes this an indefinite noun, meaning 'a punishment' rather than 'the punishment'
Root letters got vowels and ي added in middle to make it an adjective meaning 'great'
Added two dots above the end to make it 'a' great thing instead of 'the' great thing

steps.m: when (referring to تَلَقَّوْنَهُۥ - you received it)
steps.n: This is a time particle that connects to what happens next in the ayah. It shows when something happened in the past, specifically when people were receiving and spreading the false news.
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Adding ت at start makes it mean 'to receive' rather than just 'meet'
Adding ون shows multiple people are doing the action

steps.m: you (all) are receiving it (referring to the false story being spread)
steps.n: Added pronoun هُ to show what is being received. In this ayah, it refers to the false story being spread.
Added vowels and alif to form the singular noun for 'tongue'
This is the plural form of لسان (tongue)
Adds second person plural masculine possessive pronoun

steps.m: with/by your tongues (spreading the false rumor mentioned in تَلَقَّوْنَهُ)
steps.n: The preposition بِ shows how the false rumor was spread - using their tongues. This relates to the previous word تَلَقَّوْنَهُ (you received/spread it) in the ayah.
Added vowels to make the past tense form. The middle و changes to ا in this pattern.
Added تَ prefix to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense. The middle ا returns to و.
Added masculine plural suffix ون to indicate multiple addressees
Added وَ at beginning to connect this action with what came before in the verse - when you were passing it with your tongues and you were saying with your mouths...
Added short 'a' and 'silent' marks to make the basic word for 'mouth'
Changes single 'mouth' to plural form 'mouths'
Adds kasra at end to show the word is affected by a preposition
Adds 'your (plural)' to show multiple people's mouths
Adds 'with/by' to show using or doing something with mouths
This is a relative pronoun that can mean 'what' or 'that which'. No morphological changes needed as this is a basic particle form.
Added shadda for emphasis. In this ayah, this word is what's being said - the object of تَقُولُونَ (you say).
This is a special verb that only comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. It's used to negate things.
This is a special verb that always comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. In this ayah, it negates the word عِلْمٌ (knowledge), saying 'there is no knowledge of it for you.'
Added fatha (َ) to show the basic vowel of this particle

steps.m: for you all (addressing multiple people in يَقُولُونَ)
steps.n: Added كُم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people. This connects to the verb يَقُولُونَ (they say) in this ayah, showing what is being said is directed to multiple people.
Starting with the preposition ب which shows how something is done

steps.m: with it/by it (referring to what was said)
steps.n: Added the pronoun ه (him/it) to show what the preposition refers to. In this ayah, it refers back to what was being said.
Added a small alif (ۦ) after the ه as a pronunciation helper - this doesn't change the meaning
Changed the verb into a noun by adding kasra and sukun. This pattern turns an action (knowing) into the thing itself (knowledge).

steps.m: any knowledge (in مَّا لَيْسَ لَكُم بِهِۦ عِلْمٌ - what you have no knowledge of)
steps.n: The double-damma at the end shows this is talking about any knowledge in general, not specific knowledge. This fits the context where Allah is saying people are speaking about things they have no knowledge about.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense 'you' form by adding تَ prefix and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added هُ to show what is being thought about
Added وَ to connect this phrase with the previous one
The ي is doubled (has shadda) to show intensity of meaning

steps.m: easy/light (as something being described in the sentence)
steps.n: Added tanween alif to show this word is describing something in the sentence

steps.m: he/it (referring to the matter of spreading rumors)
steps.n: This is the basic pronoun meaning 'he' or 'it'. In this ayah, it refers to the matter of spreading rumors mentioned before.

steps.m: and it (referring to the matter of spreading rumors)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the start to mean 'and'. This connects this statement to what came before it.

steps.m: to be present or near something
steps.n: This is a common preposition in Arabic that comes from the idea of being present with something
Added vowel marks to make it work as a preposition. In this ayah, it shows that something is 'with/near Allah', connecting to the word اللَّهِ that comes after it.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity'
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is replaced with ٱ and the letters merge with shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show possession - this word is being possessed by something in the sentence
Connected the root letters with fatha to make a past tense verb
Adding ي and changing the vowels makes this describe a permanent quality rather than an action
Adding the two dots above makes the word indefinite, meaning 'a great thing' rather than 'the great thing'
Added vowel marks to show correct pronunciation: fatha on ل and sukun on و
Changed alif to alif madd (آ) to show long vowel sound
Added و with fatha at beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, like saying 'and' in English

steps.m: when (followed by سَمِعْتُمُوهُ - you heard it)
steps.n: This is a time particle that points to a specific moment in the past. In this ayah, it connects to سَمِعْتُمُوهُ (you heard it) to show when something happened.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Changed to first person past tense by adding تُ
Changed تُ (meaning 'I') to تُم (meaning 'you all') at the end
Added و at the end to show 'him' as the object being heard
Added هُ at the end to show what was heard ('it')
This is the basic concept of speaking or saying something
Command form meaning 'Say!' - made by adding a damma and sukun to the root letters
Added تُم to show that multiple people (you all) did this action in the past
This is the basic form of the word ما which can mean 'what' or 'that which'
When ما follows أَن, it gets a shadda and changes from meaning 'what' to meaning 'not'. Here it tells us something is not proper to do.
Connected the root letters together
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future
Added ُ at the end to show this is the main subject doing the action. This word is connected to قُلْتُم (you said) in the verse.
Combined the preposition ل (for/to) with the pronoun نا (us/our) and added fatha to show connection
Extended the final alif to show emphasis in recitation
This is a particle that connects two ideas and shows that what comes after it is the purpose or result of what came before. Here it introduces what they should not have said.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Added تَ at beginning and doubled the middle letter to show speaking to/with oneself
Added نَ at beginning to show 'we' are doing the action
Added shadda on ن for emphasis and smoother pronunciation after أَن
This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to something nearby. In this ayah, it's pointing to what was being said about someone's reputation.

steps.m: with this (refers to speaking about the slander)
steps.n: The prefix بِ means 'with' or 'by'. Here it shows how people shouldn't speak with/about this kind of slander.

steps.m: to move freely through space (like swimming)
steps.n: The root relates to moving freely like swimming, which metaphorically extends to glorifying God
The root letters س-ب-ح get vowels (ُ and ْ) to make سُبْح, changing from swimming motion to being perfect/flawless
Added ٰنَ at the end to turn 'being perfect' into 'glory be to' - a special way to praise Allah
Added كَ to show this describes Allah. This is a special phrase used to declare that Allah is free from any imperfection.
This is the basic demonstrative element that points to something
Added ذا to make it specifically point to something masculine and nearby. The alif elongates the sound to emphasize the pointing.

steps.m: to shock or surprise with false claims
steps.n: The root conveys the idea of being struck with astonishment, often due to false accusations
Changed from a verb root into a noun meaning 'false claim' by adding vowel marks
Added ان at the end to show intensity of the false claim
Added tanween to show this is the subject being described as عَظِيمٌ (great/grievous) in this ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into an adjective showing intensity. This word describes how big/serious the slander is.

steps.m: to advise or warn with good intentions

steps.n: Root changed to present tense verb 'he advises' - shows Allah giving advice/warning to people
Adds second person masculine plural object pronoun suffix
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel marks
Added the U mark at the end to show this is the subject
This is a basic connecting word that joins two parts of a sentence. Here it connects Allah's warning to what He's warning about (returning to similar behavior).
Added damma to create the basic verb form
Added تَ at beginning to show 'you' are doing the action

steps.m: you (all) return (warning from Allah not to)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people are being addressed. In this ayah, Allah is warning the believers not to return to something.

steps.m: to be like or similar to something

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun that means 'similar to' or 'like'
Added لِ at beginning to mean 'to' and ِ at end because it's connected to another word. This word connects to the verb تَعُودُوا (return to) in the ayah.

steps.m: to anything like it (referring to the slander mentioned earlier)
steps.n: Added هِ with extension marks (ۦٓ) to show this refers back to something mentioned before (the slander)
Connected the root letters and added fatha marks to create the basic noun form

steps.m: forever (describing how long they should not return to similar speech)
steps.n: Adding -an at the end changes this from 'eternity' to 'forever' - it now describes how long something lasts
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. In this ayah, it connects 'if' with 'you are believers', showing that what came before depends on being true believers.

steps.m: to exist or be in a state

steps.n: Added vowels (fatha) to make it past tense 'he' form
Changed vowels because of the weak middle letter و
Added تُ at the end to show 'I was' (past tense)
Changed from 'I was' (كُنْتُ) to 'you all were' (كُنتُم) by replacing ـتُ with ـتُم to make it plural, since it's talking to multiple believers

steps.m: to feel or give safety and trust

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Added alif at beginning which strengthens the meaning to show complete trust/belief
Changed verb 'believed' into a noun 'believer' by adding مُ at start and changing middle letters
Added ين at the end to make it plural (more than one believer), and added a stronger م sound (shadda) at the start
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding shadda (doubling) to middle root letter makes the meaning more intensive
Adding يُ at the start shows this is happening now or in the future. The vowel pattern changes to match the present tense form.

steps.m: and he makes very clear (to you all)
steps.n: The وَ connects this to the previous statement. This word is followed by اللَّهُ (Allah) who is doing the action, and لَكُمُ (to you all) who are receiving it.
The و means 'and' and connects this to what came before. The word means 'makes clear' or 'explains'

steps.m: a deity, one who is worshipped
steps.n: Connected the letters and added vowels to make the word for 'deity'
Changed to the unique name of The One True God by adding ال, doubling the ل with shadda, and adding ُ at the end to show this is the subject of the sentence
Added fatha to show this means 'to/for' - this particle connects to what comes after it
Added كُمُ to show this is directed to multiple people. This word is receiving the action of يُبَيِّنُ (He makes clear) in this ayah.

steps.m: to show, appear, or be clear

steps.n: Adding ة at the end turns this into a noun meaning 'a sign'
Changed from one sign to multiple signs by adding ات
Added kasra at the end to show this word is in the genitive case
This is the base word for Allah's name. It's a special word that doesn't come from a typical root.
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يُبَيِّنُ - explains/makes clear)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the doer of the action in the sentence

steps.m: and Allah (as the subject of يُبَيِّنُ - explains/makes clear)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, and added ٱ (wasl form of ال) after و
Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a strong quality
Added tanween damma to show this is describing Allah in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into an intensive adjective showing Allah's perfect wisdom. This word is one of Allah's names/attributes mentioned in this ayah.
This is a special word that doesn't come from a root. It's used at the start of sentences to make what follows stronger or more certain.

Added the kasra (ِ) under the إ and shadda (ّ) on ن. The shadda makes the ن sound stronger, which matches how this word makes the meaning stronger. This word emphasizes the phrase that follows it about 'those who' (ٱلَّذِينَ).
Added ٱلّ at the beginning to make it definite and more specific. The shadda (ّ) emphasizes the ل.

steps.m: those who (subject of يُحِبُّونَ - they love)
steps.n: Added ينَ at the end to make it plural. This word is the subject of يُحِبُّونَ (they love) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Double letter is written with shadda instead of repeating
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This word refers to 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) from earlier in the verse.

steps.m: that (introduces what those who love want to happen)
steps.n: This is a helper word that connects two ideas. Here it connects 'those who love' with what they want to happen. It shows up before يُحِبُّونَ (they love) to introduce the thing they love.
Added the basic vowel pattern to connect the root letters

The تَ at the beginning shows this is happening now or in the future. The َ at the end shows this is in the subjunctive mood because it comes after أَن (that). This word refers to الفاحشة (indecency) spreading.

steps.m: to go beyond proper limits in behavior
steps.n: The root describes something that goes beyond proper limits
Adding vowels turns the root into a past tense verb meaning 'to become indecent'
Added alif after first letter and ة at end to make it into a noun describing the action
Added ال at beginning to make it specific
Changed alif to hamzat wasl and added ُ to show it's doing the action in the sentence
This is a small word that shows where something is or happens. In this ayah, it shows that the punishment happens in this world.
The ي changes to ى when it comes at the end of a word. This is just a writing change and doesn't affect the meaning.
Added ال to make it definite. The ل gets a shadda because it's a sun letter that assimilates with ذ.

steps.m: those who (referring to those who love that immorality spreads)
steps.n: Added ين at the end to make it plural, referring to multiple people. This word connects to ءَامَنُوا۟ (believed) which comes right after it.
Added alif after first letter to strengthen the meaning from just trusting to having complete faith

steps.m: they believed (referring to الَّذِينَ - those who)
steps.n: Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action. This word refers back to الَّذِينَ (those who) mentioned earlier in the verse.
Added fatha to show this is the preposition meaning 'for' or 'to'
Added هُمْ at the end to show this is 'for them'. This word connects to عَذَابٌ (punishment) to show who will receive the punishment.
Added vowel marks to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

Changed the verb into a noun meaning 'punishment'. The double-damma (ٌ) at the end shows this is indefinite (like saying 'a punishment'). This word is connected to لَهُمْ (for them) to mean 'they have a punishment'.
Changed from basic root meaning 'pain' to adjective meaning 'painful' by adding vowel marks
Added ي to show intensity of the pain
Added double dammah to make it indefinite
This is a preposition that shows where something happens. Here it shows that the shameful deeds would spread among/within the believers (ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟)
Root د-ن-و (close/near) becomes دُنْيَا (worldly life) by adding special pattern with damma, sukun, fatha, alif to make a noun meaning 'the closest/nearest life'
Added ال to make it specific and doubled the د letter because it comes after ال
Changed to pattern آفِعل to mean 'more/most late' or 'last'
Adding ة makes it feminine and changes meaning to refer to afterlife
Adds 'and' to connect this word to previous words

steps.m: and the hereafter (connected to فِي from previous phrase)
steps.n: Added 'al' (ال) at start and kasra ending because it follows the word 'in' (في) - just like 'in this world' before it
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the subject doing the action (of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)

steps.m: and Allah (subject of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: Added 'and' (وَ) at the start to connect with previous statement
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
The يَ at the beginning shows this is happening now. The ُ at the end shows this is the subject doing the action. In this ayah, Allah is the one who knows.
Starting with the basic singular pronoun 'you'
Adding مْ (with a damma on the ت) changes 'you' from one person to many people
Adding وَ at the beginning connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'

This is a negative particle that makes the verb تَعْلَمُونَ (you know) into 'you do not know'. Unlike English where 'not' often comes after helping verbs like 'do', in Arabic لا comes before the verb it negates.
Basic past tense verb form with fatha vowels
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ

steps.m: you all know/have knowledge (while وَٱللَّهُ يَعْلَمُ - and Allah knows)
steps.n: Added ونَ to show multiple people are doing the action. This word contrasts with Allah's knowledge in the ayah.

steps.m: if not for (introduces condition for فَضْلُ)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a hypothetical scenario. Here it introduces what would happen without Allah's favor (فَضْلُ)

steps.m: and if not for (introduces condition for فَضْلُ)
steps.n: The و connects this conditional statement with what came before it

steps.m: to give extra or do more than required

steps.n: Changes from verb (to give extra) to noun (favor/bounty)
Added ُ at the end to show this is the subject of لَوْلَا (if not for). The full phrase means 'if not for Allah's favor...'
Added shadda to emphasize this is the supreme, ultimate deity
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and alif became a wasla (ٱ) because it's connected to the previous word
This is a basic preposition showing something being on top of or affecting something else, like saying 'on' or 'upon' in English
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for attaching the next part. This is just a spelling change and doesn't change the meaning.
Added كُمْ which means 'you all' when talking to a group of people. This shows who is being affected by the preposition.

steps.m: to be merciful, to show kindness

steps.n: Changed the root into a noun meaning 'mercy' by adding vowels and ة at the end
Added و at the beginning to connect this word with the previous word فَضْلُ (favor/grace), showing they go together like saying 'grace and mercy'
Added هُۥ at the end to show this mercy belongs to Allah, like saying 'His mercy'
This is a particle that emphasizes what comes after it and connects it to what came before, like saying 'that' or 'indeed' in English
The conjunction وَ is added to connect this with what came before
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'one who is worshipped'
This is the unique name for the One True God in Islam. The ال makes it specific and the doubled ل shows the specialness of this name.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
The و in the middle makes it an intensive form, showing someone who does this action a lot
The ٌ (tanween) at the end shows this is describing Allah as extremely kind and merciful, often paired with رَّحِيمٌ (most merciful)
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb about feeling mercy
Changed from a past-tense verb (he showed mercy) to a describing word (merciful) by adding ي in the middle and ٌ at the end
Added shadda (ّ) on first letter to show this is an intense, perfect form of mercy that only Allah has

steps.m: to point to or indicate something
which/what (asking about or pointing to something)
steps.n: Connects the root letters with vowels and adds shadda to make a word for asking about or pointing to things
Adds يا with elongated alif to transform the word into a way of calling attention
Adds ها with ُ to make it a way to address people
This is a special demonstrative pronoun used to refer to people or things being discussed
Adding آ (alif with madd) to the root changes it from 'feeling safe' to 'choosing to trust and believe in something'
The وا at the end shows this was done by a group of people. This word describes 'those who' (الَّذِينَ) from earlier in the verse.
This is a negative particle that comes before verbs to tell people not to do something. Here it comes before تَتَّبِعُوا۟ (follow) to say 'don't follow'
Added fatha mark to help with pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Adds ت and doubles middle letter to show doing something with extra effort/intensity

steps.m: you (all) intensely follow (negative command: 'do not follow')

Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'a step' by adding the vowel marks ُ (u) and ْ (sukoon)
Changed from خُطْو by adding ُ (damma) after the second letter to make it a basic noun form
Added ة to show one instance of stepping
Changed to plural form by adding ات and replacing the ة
Added kasra at the end to show this word belongs to something (in this case, it belongs to Satan/الشيطان)

steps.m: to rebel, to be distant from good

Added ا to make it mean 'the one who rebels' (like Satan/devil)
Added ال to make it definite and doubled the ش with shadda because it comes after ال
Added kasra at the end to show this word is owned by خُطُوٰتِ (footsteps), making it mean 'footsteps of the devil'
This is a question word that became a relative pronoun meaning 'whoever'. It introduces the person who might follow in يَتَّبِعْ (follows).
Added و with fatha to connect this condition with what came before it. This introduces a new statement about what happens to whoever follows Satan's footsteps.
Adding vowels turns the root into a complete past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowels
Added ت after يَ and doubled the ب to show doing action for oneself

steps.m: he follows closely/intensely (in a conditional phrase with مَن)

Added damma and sukun to make it into a noun meaning 'one step'
Changed the middle vowel to damma to prepare for plural form
Added feminine plural ending ٰت to make complete plural form
Added kasra to show this word is possessed by الشيطان (Satan) in the phrase خطوات الشيطان (footsteps of Satan)

steps.m: to be far away in rebellion
he rebelled and went far away
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make it a past tense verb
Added ي with sukun after first root letter to show someone who does this action intensely
Added ا after second root letter to create a noun for someone characterized by intense rebellion
Added ٱل to specify the particular devil, and doubled the ش with shadda because it comes after ٱل
Changed the ending to show 'of' or 'belonging to' the devil/satan
Starting with the emphatic particle إنّ
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before

steps.m: for indeed he/it (referring to الشَّيْطٰنِ - Satan)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to refer back to Satan mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: for indeed he/it (referring to الشَّيْطٰنِ - Satan)
steps.n: Added the small و mark (ۥ) after هُ which is just a pronunciation helper that doesn't change the meaning
This root represents the basic concept of commanding or ordering
Connected the three root letters أ-م-ر together and added a damma (u sound) on the middle letter to make it mean 'I command'
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense, sukun on first letter for pronunciation, and ُ at end to show the doer. This word explains what 'he' (referring back to فَإِنَّهُۥ) does.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Changed the verb into an abstract noun by adding اء at the end and putting sukun on the middle letter
Adding ال makes the word definite
Added preposition ب with kasra to show 'with'. This word is what is being commanded in the verb يَأْمُرُ (he commands)
The words are essentially the same - just written with slightly different spelling. Both have kasra on ب

steps.m: to reject or disapprove of something

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters
Changed the word to show the thing that is rejected or disapproved of
Added ال to make it specific
Added و to connect with previous word (الفحشاء)

steps.m: and the wrong deed (object of يَأْمُرُ)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it's the object of the verb يَأْمُرُ (he commands)

steps.m: if not for (introduces a hypothetical scenario)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces hypothetical scenarios. It's used to say 'if it weren't for X, then Y would happen'

steps.m: and if not for (connects to previous statement about following Satan's footsteps)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with the previous statement. This shows that what follows is related to the warning about following Satan's footsteps.

steps.m: to give extra, to favor, to be generous

steps.n: The root letters are joined together to make a noun meaning 'a gift' or 'favor' that someone receives
The ُ at the end shows this word is the subject of لَوْلَا (if not for). This word is connected to Allah (الله) right after it to show it's specifically Allah's favor being discussed.
Added ال and merged letters to create the unique name for The One True God. The hamza is dropped and the lam gets a shadda.
Added kasra at the end to show possession, connecting it to the word فَضْلُ (bounty) in the ayah. Also changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes in the middle of speech.

steps.m: upon (preposition showing something affecting فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or affecting something else. In this ayah, it's showing Allah's favor affecting or being upon people.
The ى changes to يْ before adding pronouns at the end. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
The suffix كُمْ means 'you all' when added to the end. It shows this applies to a group of people being spoken to.
Changed the word into a noun meaning 'mercy' by adding ة at the end and vowel marks
The ُ shows this word is the subject doing the action of 'would purify' (زَكَىٰ) in the ayah
Added وَ (and) to connect this with the previous word فضل (grace) - together they form 'grace and mercy'

steps.m: and His mercy (subject of would purify)
steps.n: Added هُ (His) to show this is Allah's mercy. The ۥ mark is added to extend the sound of هُ
This is a negative particle that makes the verb after it negative. In this ayah, it negates زَكَىٰ (purified), making the meaning 'not purified' or 'would never have been purified'

steps.m: to purify, make grow, or become pure

steps.n: Added fathas to show this happened in the past. The و at the end changes to ى because it's a weak letter at the end of a word.
Added the small alif (ٰ) after ى to show it's a long vowel sound at the end
This is a preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or someone. In this ayah, it's used with أَحَدٍ (anyone) to mean 'from anyone'

steps.m: from you all (connected to زَكَىٰ - would be purified)
steps.n: Added كُم which means 'you all' to show who the word 'from' refers to. This connects to the verb زَكَىٰ (would be purified) to show who would be purified.
This is a basic preposition that shows something comes from somewhere or belongs to someone. Here it connects to the word أَحَدٍ (anyone) to mean 'from anyone'.
The doubled letter (shadda) makes the meaning stronger, emphasizing that not a single person would have been purified.
Root letters connected with fat-ha vowels to make the word mean 'one' or 'anyone'
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this word is being acted upon by the word مِن (from) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic noun
Adding tanween alif makes it describe how something happens, like an adverb in English

steps.m: but (contrasting with ما زَكَىٰ - 'none would be pure')
steps.n: This is a particle that introduces a contrast with what came before. Here it contrasts with the previous statement that 'none would be pure' by introducing Allah's ability to purify.

steps.m: and but (connecting to ما زَكَىٰ and contrasting with it)
steps.n: Adds 'and' to connect this contrast with the previous statement about none being pure without Allah's favor
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word in Arabic that always refers to The One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being acted upon)
steps.n: Added fatha at the end to show Allah is being acted upon in this sentence. Also added shadda on ل and changed initial alif to wasla form since it comes after another word.

steps.m: to purify or make something grow better

steps.n: Connected the letters together with fatha vowels to make it a past tense verb
The doubled middle letter (shadda) makes the meaning stronger. The final و changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.
Adding يُ at the beginning shows this is happening now or will happen. The middle vowel changes to ِ (kasra) as part of this pattern.
This is a special word used to refer to people in a general way. In this ayah, it refers to whoever Allah purifies (يُزَكِّى).
Adding the fatha (َ) at the end makes it a complete past tense verb
Changes from past tense (he wanted) to present tense (he wants/wills) by adding يَ at the start
This is a unique proper name that doesn't follow normal word patterns. It's the name of God in Arabic and is considered sacred.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing something in the sentence
Added و at the beginning to mean 'and', and added ٱ (alif with wasl) which helps connect the words smoothly when speaking
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past tense verb
Changes from 'he heard' to 'one who hears perfectly' by using the pattern fa'eel (سَمِيع) to show Allah's complete hearing ability
Adding vowel marks to make it a past tense verb meaning 'to know'
Changed from 'he knew' (past action) to 'All-Knowing' (permanent quality) by using the pattern with ي

steps.m: All-Knowing (as a general attribute of Allah)

steps.m: the bounty/favor (possessed by أُو۟لُوا۟ - people of)
steps.n: Adding definite article makes the noun definite
Adding kasra for genitive case

Basic meaning of the root

Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing middle vowels

Added َ at end to show this is the object being acted upon

Adds definite article ٱلْ after the conjunction وَ

steps.m: upon (preposition showing something is on top of or against something else)

steps.m: upon them (testifying against them in this ayah)
steps.n: Added هِمْ which means 'them' (for a group). In this ayah, it shows their body parts will testify against them.

Added conjunction وَ (and) at the beginning
