The root forms a noun meaning 'sky' or 'heaven'
Added vowels to connect the root letters into a basic past-tense verb
Added اء to make it a noun meaning 'sky' - the place that is elevated above us
Added ٱل to make it specific and doubled the س with shadda because it comes after ٱل
Added kasra at the end because it comes after the word مِنَ (from) in the ayah. Also lengthened the alif to آ for proper writing.
Basic preposition showing where the hail comes from - in this case, from mountains in the sky. This connects what comes before (mountains) with what comes after (hail).
This root refers to something large and firm, especially a mountain
Connected the letters together with fatha marks to form the basic noun
Changes the word to show many mountains by changing the vowel pattern and adding alif
Adds tanween kasra to show this word comes after the word مِن (from) in the ayah
Basic preposition showing location or being inside something

steps.m: in it (referring to the mountains mentioned in جِبَالٍ)
steps.n: Added هَا at the end which means 'it' (feminine). Here it refers back to the mountains (جِبَالٍ) mentioned in the verse.

steps.m: from (connects جِبَالٍ 'mountains' to يُنَزِّلُ 'He sends down')
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from. In this ayah, it connects 'mountains' (جِبَالٍ) to the verb 'He sends down' (يُنَزِّلُ), showing that the hail comes from mountains in the sky.
Added fatha vowels to connect the letters and form the noun for hail
Added two dots under the last letter to show 'of hail' - like saying 'made of hail' or 'containing hail'

steps.m: to hit or reach a target accurately

steps.n: Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at the start and changing the middle vowels. The word means 'to hit/strike something'

steps.m: and then he strikes (referring to hail striking whom Allah wills)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this happens as a result of the hail being sent down mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a preposition that shows how something is done

steps.m: with it/by means of it (object of فَيُصِيبُ)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ (it/him) to show what is being affected. This word connects to فَيُصِيبُ (then He strikes) in the ayah, showing what Allah uses to strike.

steps.m: with it/by means of it (object of فَيُصِيبُ)
steps.n: Added ۦ which is just a pronunciation mark that doesn't change the meaning
This is a relative pronoun that refers to people. In this ayah, it refers to whoever Allah chooses to strike with the hail mentioned earlier.
The root letters ش-ي-أ became شَاءَ (he wanted) by adding vowels and changing ي to ا
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action
Added long ا to show extended duration of wanting/wishing
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now, with ُ at the end to show 'he' is doing it
Added هُ at the end to show what is being turned away. This connects to the previous words about hail (بَرَدٍ) in the ayah.

steps.m: and he turns it away (connected to يُنَزِّلُ - He sends down)
steps.n: Added وَ (and) at the beginning to connect this action with the previous action of Allah sending down hail from the sky
This is a basic preposition that shows movement away from something. In this ayah, it connects the verb يَصْرِفُهُ (He turns it away) with مَن (whom), showing who the action affects.
This is a relative pronoun used to refer to people. The shadda shows emphasis.
Changed from basic root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding vowels. The middle root letter (ي) becomes an alif (ا)
Added يَ at beginning to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action
Added long ا to show extended duration of wanting/wishing

steps.m: to almost do something/to be on the verge of doing something

steps.n: The و (weak letter) changes to ا when we add the vowels. This is a common change in Arabic for these types of letters.

Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now or in the future. Added ُ at the end because this word is doing the action in the sentence - it's describing how the flash (سَنَا) of lightning almost takes away sight.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Changes from a verb (he flashed) to a noun (flash/brilliance)
Root meaning 'flash' becomes the noun 'lightning' by adding the vowels
Added kasra to show this lightning belongs to something

steps.m: his lightning (referring to Allah's lightning in this ayah)
steps.n: Added هِۦ to show this lightning belongs to Him (Allah)
This is the basic meaning showing movement away from a place
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past

steps.m: he goes away (describes what the lightning's flash almost does to vision)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, and ُ at end. In this ayah, this word describes how lightning's flash nearly takes away people's vision.
Root letters with vowels added to make a noun meaning 'sight/vision'
Changed from singular 'sight' to plural 'sights' by adding alif at start
Adding ال (al) at the start to mean 'the'
Basic past tense verb form of root meaning 'turn/change' - like saying 'he turned'
Doubling the middle letter shows doing the action many times (turning over and over)
Changes from past to present tense - starts with يُ (yu) and has damma (ُ) at end to show it's happening now
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns

steps.m: Allah (the one true God) (subject of يُقَلِّبُ)
steps.n: Changed to the unique name of the One God by adding ال and merging letters. The ُ shows this is the doer of the action يُقَلِّبُ (alternates) in this verse.
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukun on second letter to make the basic noun. This word is what's being alternated in the phrase 'Allah alternates the night'.

steps.m: the night (object of يُقَلِّبُ - He alternates)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning to make it specific and fatha at end to show it's being acted upon. The shadda appears on the ل when ال is added to a word starting with ل.
The words are the same - both have hamzat wasl (ٱ) at the beginning. The only difference is in the shadda marking on the lam (ل)
Connected the root letters with fathas to make it a past tense verb
Added alif after second root letter to create noun meaning 'daytime' - named because of how daylight flows across the sky
Added al- to make it specific. The ل combines with ن to make a doubled ن (shown by ّ)
Added و meaning 'and' at start. The اَل changes to ٱل after و

steps.m: and the daytime (being alternated by Allah)
steps.n: Added fatha at end to show this is the object being acted upon in يُقَلِّبُ ٱللَّهُ (Allah alternates)
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle that strengthens what comes after it
The shadda (ّ) doubles the ن, making the emphasis stronger. This particle introduces the important phrase لَعِبْرَةً (surely a lesson) in this ayah.
This is a preposition that shows something is inside or within something else. Here it shows that there is a lesson within 'that' (the alternation of night and day mentioned before).
The alif with overbar (ٰ) changes 'this' to 'that', showing the thing being pointed to is farther away
Added لِ which helps connect to the next part. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with pronunciation.

steps.m: that (referring to how Allah alternates night and day)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to make it mean 'that' - like pointing to something far away

steps.m: to cross over/pass through (especially to learn from something)
a lesson (something learned from crossing/passing through experience)
steps.n: Changes the action of crossing/passing through into the wisdom gained from that experience

steps.m: surely a lesson (that follows from ذٰلِكَ/that)
steps.n: Adds emphasis with لَ showing importance of the lesson, and ً showing it's any/a lesson. This word explains what 'that' (ذٰلِكَ) mentioned before contains.
This is a special word used to show possession or ownership. It connects to the next word (ٱلْأَبْصٰرِ) to show who possesses insight/vision.
Added لِ at the beginning to show this is 'for' or 'to' the possessors. Like saying 'for people of wisdom' in English.
Added shadda on the ل to show emphasis. This happens because there are actually two ل letters that combine together.

steps.m: to see or perceive with eyes

steps.n: Adding fatha vowels turns the root into a simple noun meaning 'sight'
Changed to broken plural form by adding أَ at start and ا after second root letter

steps.m: the sights/visions (possessed by or belonging to something)
steps.n: Added ال for definiteness and kasra to show possession or belonging
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity/god
Added shadda to emphasize this is the one true God, not just any deity
Added damma at the end to show Allah is doing the action (creating) in this sentence
Added 'wa' (and) at the beginning to connect this to what came before
Added fathas to connect the letters and show this happened in the past. This word tells what Allah (وَٱللَّهُ) did.

steps.m: to be complete, whole, or all-encompassing

steps.n: Added damma and doubled the middle letter to create a word meaning 'every' or 'all'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of the verb خَلَقَ (created)
Doubling the middle letter adds intensity to the crawling action
Changes from verb (to crawl) to noun (a crawler/creature)
Added tanwin kasra to show indefiniteness. This word is the object of خَلَقَ (He created) in this ayah.
Basic preposition showing origin or source. In this ayah, it's used to show what different creatures are made from (مَّآءٍ - water).
The shadda is added because this word comes after a word ending in ن or tanween (نْ sound), causing the initial م to be emphasized in pronunciation.
Basic root meaning related to water and fluidity
The و and ه change to اء because they are weak letters at the end of the word
Added emphasis (shadda) to first letter
Added long alif to stretch the sound for emphasis
This is a basic preposition that shows where something starts or comes from

steps.m: then/so from (connecting to خَلَقَ - He created)
steps.n: The فَ shows this follows from Allah creating all creatures from water, mentioned in the previous part

steps.m: then from them (referring to the creatures)
steps.n: The هُم means 'them' and refers back to the creatures (دَآبَّةٍ) mentioned earlier. The ْ (sukun) is added to connect the letters smoothly.
Basic relative pronoun that introduces a description of different creatures. Here it connects to يَمْشِى (walks/crawls) to describe which creatures are being talked about.
Added shadda for emphasis and smooth pronunciation when connected to previous words. This is a common Quranic style but doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to walk, to move on foot
steps.n: Basic root meaning related to walking

steps.m: he walks (one of the creatures Allah created)
steps.n: Transforms root into present tense verb with 3rd person masculine singular subject. This word describes how some of Allah's creatures move on two legs.
The ى at the end shows this is a present tense verb. It helps extend the 'ee' sound at the end.
Basic preposition showing something is positioned above or on top of something else. Here it connects with يَمْشِى (walks) to show what the creature walks on.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show proper pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Basic root meaning related to interior or belly
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this belly belongs to someone
Added هِۦ at the end to show the belly belongs to 'him' (referring back to مَن - whoever)
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it

steps.m: and from them (referring to the creatures mentioned in يَمْشِى)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show who is being referred to - in this case, it refers back to the creatures that walk mentioned in the verse
Basic relative pronoun that introduces a description of which type of creature is being discussed. It connects to the verb يَمْشِى (walks) to mean 'whoever walks'
Added shadda (doubling mark) on م to add emphasis to the word, making it stronger like saying 'the one who' instead of just 'who'
Basic meaning of walking or moving on foot
Added vowel marks to the root letters to make it mean 'he walked' in the past
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on م, and kasra on ش
Added ى at the end to complete the present tense verb form
This is a preposition showing position or location above something. In this ayah, it shows the creatures crawling 'on' their bellies.
Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end which is just a spelling variation of ى. This doesn't change the meaning.
Basic meaning related to feet or walking
Adding the vowel 'i' and sukoon makes it mean 'a foot/leg' instead of just the root meaning
The يْنِ ending shows this means exactly two, and the word comes after على (on/upon)

steps.m: from/among (preposition connecting to مَّن يَمْشِى who walks)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition that shows where something comes from or what group it belongs to
Added هُم to show we're talking about multiple things mentioned before (the creatures)
Added و to connect this phrase to what came before, showing another category of creatures
This is a word that points to people or beings who do something. Here it refers to those who walk on four legs.
The م gets doubled (shadda) because it comes right after a word ending in ن. This is a common rule in Arabic where ن + م = مّ
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the past tense verb. The final ي changes to ى because it's at the end of the word.

steps.m: he walks (describing what some creatures do)
steps.n: Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now. Changed the middle vowel to ِ (kasra) and added ْ (sukun) on first letter to match present tense pattern.
This is a primitive particle (حرف جر) that is not derived from a root. It has no morphological transformation as it is one of the basic building blocks of Arabic grammar.
Added an alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show this is a permanent feature of this word. This doesn't change the meaning, it just helps with proper writing.
Added a small alif (ٓ) above the word. This is just a writing mark that doesn't change the meaning.

steps.m: to be in groups of four
steps.n: This is the basic concept of having four parts or being fourth
Adding أ at start makes this the common word for the number 4

steps.m: four (being walked upon by creatures in يَمْشِى)
steps.n: Added tanwin kasra (two dots under) to show it's connected to the word 'walks/crawls' - means 'on four legs'
This is the basic meaning showing the action of creating something
Connected the letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start, sukun on first root letter, damma on second root letter, and damma at end. This word is connected to يَشَآءُ (he wills) in this ayah.
The words are the same - just written slightly differently. Both mean 'he creates' in present tense
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding specific vowel patterns
Added the u-vowel at the end to show this is the subject
Added 'the' at the start to make it specific/definite
This is a relative pronoun that refers to whatever Allah creates. In this ayah, it connects to the verb يَخْلُقُ (He creates) to mean 'whatever He wills'
Added fatha after each root letter. Because this is a special type of word, the middle letter ي changes to ا and the final letter أ changes to ء.
Added يَ at the beginning to show it's happening now and ُ at the end to show 'he' is doing it. The ا is written as آ when it comes after شَ in this word.
This is a special word called a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it. It has no root because it's a helper word that connects and modifies other words.
Added the kasra (ِ) and shadda (ّ) marks to show this word strongly emphasizes the next word (اللَّهَ) in the sentence.
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for the One True God.
Added shadda on ل, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show this is the object of إِنَّ (indeed/verily)
This is a preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else. In this ayah, it connects the verb يَمْشِى (walks) with what is being walked on (belly/two feet/four).
The small alif (ىٰ) at the end is just a writing style used when this word appears at certain positions in a sentence. It doesn't change the meaning.
Added damma and doubled the ل to show it means absolutely everything with no exceptions
Added kasra at the end to show this word is connected to دَآبَّةٍ (creature) that comes after it, like saying 'of all creatures'
The root letters are connected with َ (fatha) on first letter and ْ (sukun) on second letter. The final hamza (ء) stays separate. This forms a noun meaning 'thing'.
The ي is written as ى when followed by hamza. The tanwin kasratayn (ٍ) is added at the end because this word is possessed by كُلِّ (every/all) in the phrase كُلِّ شَىْءٍ (every thing).
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowel marks to create an intensive adjective showing complete possession of this quality

steps.m: All-Powerful (describing Allah's attribute of complete power over all things)
steps.n: The tanween (ٌ) shows this is describing Allah's general attribute of power over everything mentioned in the ayah (the creatures that walk on their bellies, two legs, and four legs)
قَدْ is a particle used to emphasize that something definitely happened
Added لَ at the beginning to make the meaning stronger
Added shadda on ل to make the emphasis even stronger. This word emphasizes the verb أَنزَلْنَآ (We sent down) in this ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb

steps.m: he caused to descend/he sent down (acting on ءَايٰتٍ)
steps.n: Adding أَ at start makes the verb causative - showing someone actively sending something down. This verb acts on ءَايٰتٍ (signs/verses) in this ayah.
Changed from 'he sent down' to 'we sent down' by adding نا at the end. The نا means 'we' and shows who did the action.

steps.m: to show, make clear, or point to

steps.n: Forms the singular noun meaning 'sign' or 'verse' that points to something
Changes the singular word into plural by removing ة and adding ات
Adds the genitive case marker with nunation at the end

steps.m: clear signs (object of أَنزَلْنَآ - we sent down)
steps.n: The alif after yaa is written as a small alif above (ٰ). Still means 'signs' and has tanwin kasra (-in) to show it receives the action.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added مُ prefix and changed vowels to make it describe who/what does the action
Added ات to make it feminine plural, referring to the signs/verses (آيات) mentioned earlier

steps.m: making clear ones (feminine plural, describing آيات/verses)
steps.n: Added shadda on م and tanwin kasra at end to show this describes the verses mentioned before
This is a unique word that comes from the root meaning of divinity but has become the proper name of God in Arabic. The double ل with shadda is a special feature of this name.
Added shadda on ل to show it is doubled, and fatha on it to show pronunciation
Added damma at the end to show this is the subject doing the action in the sentence - Allah is the one who guides

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَهْدِى/guides)
steps.n: Added و with fatha at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it, like saying 'and'

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَهْدِى/guides)
steps.n: Changed the regular alif to a waṣla alif (ٱ) because it connects in pronunciation with the و before it
Connected the root letters and added fathas. The ي at the end changes to ى because it's at the end of a word.
Added يَ at start to show present tense. The middle letter gets a sukun (ْ) and the last root letter gets a kasra (ِ) in present tense. This word describes what Allah does.
This is a special word that introduces a conditional or relative clause. Here it refers to the people who Allah guides, connecting to the verb يَشَآءُ (He wills/wants).
The root letters were connected and the middle letter ي became ا to make 'he wanted'
Added يَ at start to show present tense and ُ at end to show he is doing the action
Added long ا to show the extended nature of wanting/willing
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. Here it connects to the word صِرٰطٍ (path) to show movement towards that path.
Added kasra under إ and an alif khanjariya (small alif) above the ى to help with pronunciation. This doesn't change the meaning.
Added vowel marks to create the basic noun form
Changed the regular alif to an elongated alif (ٰ) which is common in this word
Added tanwin kasra to show this is indefinite (like 'a path') and comes after the word إِلَىٰ (to)
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters

steps.m: one who/that which is continuously straight/upright (emphasized)
steps.n: Changed from basic verb 'to be straight' into a special pattern (مستقيم) that means 'continuously straight'. The shadda is just from pronunciation rules, not meaning.
Shows this word describes the path (صِرٰطٍ) mentioned earlier in the ayah
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense form
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle و stays because it's part of the root.
Adding ون shows that multiple people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action
Adding و at the start connects this word to what came before it, like saying 'and'
Changed root 'to feel safe' into 'he believed' by adding alif after hamza - this is how we make Form IV verbs that mean 'to make something happen'

steps.m: we believed (subject of يَقُولُونَ - they say)
steps.n: Added نَّا at the end to show 'we' are doing the action. This word is what 'they say' in the verse.
Added shadda on ل to show emphasis in pronunciation of Allah's name
Changed initial alif to waslah alif (ٱ) since it connects with previous word

steps.m: in/by/with Allah (connected to ءَامَنَّا - we believed)
steps.n: Added بِ at start to mean 'in/by/with' and ِ at end to show connection. This shows what or who they believed in, connecting to the word ءَامَنَّا (we believed) in the ayah.
The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot
Added ال to make it specific. The ر gets doubled (shadda) because it's a sun letter that comes after ال
Added بِ at the beginning to show how something is done or the means by which it happens. Added ِ at the end because it follows the word بِ

steps.m: and in/by the messenger (connected to ءَامَنَّا)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this with the previous phrase about believing in Allah
Added vowel marks and connected letters. The middle letter و changes to ا in past tense.
Added أ at beginning and changed middle ا to sukun to make it a command ("Obey!")
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action
Added وَ at beginning to connect this word with the previous statement
Added damma (ُ) to show this is a word that connects events in sequence

steps.m: then, thereafter (connecting to يَتَوَلَّىٰ - turns away)
steps.n: Added shadda (ّ) with fatha to emphasize the separation between events. In this ayah, it shows the gap between claiming faith and then turning away.

steps.m: to be close to/take charge of something

steps.n: Adding shadda doubles the middle letter to intensify the meaning
Added ت to show the person is doing this action to themselves
Changed from past tense (he turned away) to present tense (he turns away) by adding يَ at the start
Added alif maksura at the end to show this is the final form of the word

steps.m: to split or divide into groups

steps.n: Adding ي and kasra (ِ) to the root changes it from 'to split' into 'a group that split away' - like how a team breaks away from the main group
The tanween (ٌ) shows this is any group, not a specific one
This is a preposition meaning 'from'. Since it's a particle, we start with its basic form rather than a root.
The ن gets a shadda (doubled) because it comes before another م in the next word of the ayah (مِّنۢ بَعْدِ). This is just a pronunciation change.
Added sukun on the ن to show it has no vowel
Added هُم at the end to show this means 'them'. In this ayah, it refers to the group that turns away after claiming to believe.
Basic preposition showing starting point or origin

The shadda (ّ) appears because this word comes after a word ending in ن or م, causing the م to be doubled in pronunciation. The small ن (ۢ) at the end is a pronunciation mark showing the letter should be clear.
Connected the root letters with vowel marks to make a noun meaning 'after'
Added kasra at the end to show this word is possessed by ذٰلِكَ (that), making the phrase 'after that'
Basic demonstrative pronoun for pointing to something nearby
The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) to show distance
Added لِ to strengthen the meaning of distance

steps.m: that (referring to what was just mentioned)
steps.n: Added كَ at the end to show this points to something mentioned in the previous statement. Here it refers to the group's claim of belief and obedience that they then turn away from.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation
Changed ا to آ which is just a writing variation that means the same thing
Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. This word negates the verb يَقُولُونَ (they say) in this ayah.
Basic demonstrative pronoun form with damma and fatha to show it points to something far away
Added hamza and kasra to show we're pointing to something far away
Added كَ at the end to show we're addressing someone or pointing something out to them, like saying 'those ones there' to someone

steps.m: to trust, feel safe, be faithful

steps.n: Adds مُ at the start to show who does the action (like adding -er in English), with ؤ replacing أ because it comes after ُ
Adds ين to show we're talking about multiple believers
Adds ال to point to specific believers being talked about, with َ on the end because it's affected by another word

steps.m: with the believers (connected to ءَامَنَّا - we believed)
steps.n: Adds بِ to show connection with ءَامَنَّا (we believed) earlier in the verse, meaning we believed along with these believers

steps.m: when (introduces the condition of being called to Allah and His Messenger)
steps.n: This is a conditional particle that introduces a time condition. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when the hypocrites are called to Allah and His Messenger for judgment.

steps.m: and when (introduces the condition of being called to Allah and His Messenger)
steps.n: The و connects this condition to what came before it in the previous ayah. When إِذَا comes after و, it often shows a sequence of events or conditions.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The و changes to ا at the end because it's a weak letter.
Changed to passive voice by changing vowels to damma then kasra. The weak letter و becomes ي in this pattern.

steps.m: they were called (to Allah and His Messenger)
steps.n: Added وا to show multiple people. The ي changes back to و, and the middle vowel changes to match. The small marks (۟) show this is plural.
This is a basic preposition showing direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects 'they were called' (دُعُوٓا۟) with 'Allah' (ٱللَّهِ), showing who they were called to.
Added ال and merged it with the word, changing the first letter. This makes it refer specifically to Allah, the One True God, rather than just any deity.
Added shadda on ل to emphasize it, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added kasra at the end because it follows the word إِلَى (to) in this ayah.
The و after س makes it mean 'one who does this action a lot'
Added هِ (hi) at the end to show this messenger belongs to Allah (like saying 'Allah's messenger'). The ِ (kasra) before it shows this word connects to another word

steps.m: and His messenger (referring to Allah's messenger)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with 'Allah' from earlier in the verse, showing both are being called to
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
The يَ at the start changes it from past tense (he judged) to present tense (he judges/is judging)

steps.m: so that he may judge (purpose for دُعُوٓا۟/they were called)
steps.n: The لِ shows this is the purpose or reason for being called. This word explains why they were called in دُعُوٓا۟ (they were called).

steps.m: to be separate/distinct from something else

steps.n: Forms a preposition meaning 'between' from the root meaning of separation. This word shows what Allah's Messenger will judge between.

steps.m: between them (object of يَحْكُمَ - to judge)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being judged between - in this case, different groups of people mentioned in the verse
This is a basic time particle that refers to a point in time
Adding ا changes it from just 'when' to 'whenever/if' - like saying 'at the time when' instead of just 'when'. It connects to the word 'they are called' (دُعُوٓا۟) that comes after it

steps.m: to separate or split things apart

steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to a past tense verb meaning 'became separated' by adding vowel marks
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this describes a group that has this quality

steps.m: a group (that is separated from others)
steps.n: Added tanween damma to show this is talking about any group, not a specific one
This is a basic preposition showing source or origin
Added هُم at the end to show who is being referred to
Added shadda on م for pronunciation emphasis because it comes after و in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action
Letter م has a shadda (double mark) because it comes after a word ending in ن or م

steps.m: those who turn away (subject of إِذَا فَرِيقٌ)
steps.n: Added ون to show this describes multiple people. This word describes فَرِيقٌ (a group) in the ayah.

steps.m: if (introduces a condition for يَكُن 'there is')
steps.n: This is a conditional word that introduces a hypothetical situation. In this ayah, it introduces the condition 'if there is truth for them'

steps.m: and if (connects to previous statement and introduces condition for يَكُن 'there is')
steps.n: The و connects this condition to what came before it, showing these are related ideas
Added vowels (fat-ha) to make it past tense 'he was'
Changed from past tense (كَوَنَ) to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing middle vowel - like changing 'was' to 'is'
When إِن comes before this word, it removes the 'u' sound at the end and shortens it from yakoonu to yakun
Combines the preposition ل (for/to) with the pronoun هم (them)
The ل gets a shadda (doubled) to add emphasis, and هم gets a final ُ because it connects to the verb يَأْتُوٓا۟ (they come) in this ayah
Added fatha and doubled the middle letter to create a noun meaning the essence of being true
Added ال to specify this is THE truth being discussed in this context
Added damma to show this is the subject of يَكُن (to be) in the phrase 'if the truth is theirs'
This is the basic concept of coming or arriving
Added يَ at the beginning to show someone is doing this action now or in the future

steps.m: they come/are coming (to إِلَيْهِ - to him)
steps.n: Changed to show multiple people by adding وا at the end. This word connects to إِلَيْهِ (to him) in the ayah, showing where they are coming to.
Adding sukun (ْ) on the middle letter helps show this is a basic verb form
Basic preposition indicating direction or destination
Changed the final ى to ي with sukun to prepare for connecting with the next part. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect meaning.
Added هِ meaning 'him/it' to show who is being approached. This connects to يَأْتُوٓا۟ (they come) to show who they come to.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Added مُ at start and changed vowels to show someone who does this action
Added ين to show this refers to multiple people

steps.m: those who submit willingly (describing their state in يَأْتُوٓا۟)
steps.n: Added َ at the end to show this describes how they come (يَأْتُوٓا۟) in the verse
Adding the fatha (َ) shows this is a question word that turns a statement into a question, like adding a question mark in English
Adding فَ shows that this question is about something that follows or results from something else

steps.m: Is there then in...? (questioning what is inside/within something)
steps.n: Adding ى with kasra (ِ) shows this question is about something being inside or within something else. In this ayah, it's asking about what's in their hearts.

steps.m: heart (as the thing that turns feelings and thoughts)

steps.n: Connected the letters together to make the basic word for 'heart'
Changes from single heart (qalb) to plural hearts (qulūb) by changing the vowels inside the word
Adds هِم at the end to show the hearts belong to them
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the noun form
Added tanween dammah to show this is any general sickness (not a specific one)

steps.m: a severe sickness/disease (in قُلُوبِهِم their hearts)
steps.n: The م (meem) has a shaddah, making it stronger/emphasized to show severe sickness. This word tells us about what's in their hearts.
This is a basic questioning word used to connect two alternative questions, like 'is it this... or that?' In this ayah, it connects 'Is there a disease in their hearts?' with 'or do they fear?'
The sukun changes to kasra because the next word starts with a connecting hamza (ٱ in ٱرْتَابُوٓا۟). This is just to make it easier to pronounce.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added ٱ and ت to show someone actively becoming doubtful (rather than just having doubt)
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action

steps.m: or (connecting to أَفِى 'is it in' to present an alternative possibility)
steps.n: This is a questioning particle that connects to the previous question أَفِى to present two possible explanations: either there is a disease in their hearts, or they have doubts
Basic meaning of being afraid
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the past tense. The و (middle letter) changes to ا, which is common for words with و in the middle.
Added يَ at the start to show it's happening now or in the future, and changed the ending vowel to ُ

steps.m: they fear/are afraid (referring to the disbelievers in this ayah)
steps.n: Added ونَ at the end to show that many people (all male or mixed group) are doing the action. In this ayah, it refers to the disbelievers who fear Allah and His Messenger will be unfair to them.

steps.m: that (introduces what they fear will happen)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects يَخَافُونَ (they fear) to what follows. It introduces the thing that is feared.
Basic meaning of being unfair or oppressive
Added short vowels (fat-ha, kasra, fat-ha) to make it mean 'he was unfair' in the past

steps.m: he is/will be unfair (object of أَن)
steps.n: Added يَ at the beginning to show this is happening now or in the future. This verb comes after أَن (that) in the ayah.
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'a deity' by adding vowel patterns
Changed from the general word for 'a god' to the special name 'Allah' by adding ال. The ُ at the end shows Allah is doing the action in this sentence.
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on top of'. It comes from the idea of being high or elevated.
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.
Added هِمْ at the end to show who is being referred to (them).
Basic meaning of sending
Changed from action to person who does the action. The و after second letter shows this is their main role.
Added damma to show this is the subject doing something
Added هُ at the end to show this messenger belongs to Allah
Added وَ at beginning to connect this word to what came before it

steps.m: but rather (contradicting the previous suggestions of illness, doubt, or fear)
steps.n: This word shows that the previous suggestions (about having illness in their hearts, having doubts, or fearing Allah's injustice) are incorrect. Instead, it introduces the real reason that will be mentioned next.
Base form of the demonstrative pronoun for plural
Extended alif shows emphasis and distance
Added ئِ to show distance from speaker
Added كَ to show you're talking to someone about those people
Basic form of the separate pronoun meaning 'they' or 'them' for a group of males or mixed gender
Added damma at the end because this pronoun is being used for emphasis before the word الظَّالِمُونَ (the wrongdoers). It shows that THEY specifically are the wrongdoers.
Basic meaning of doing wrong
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Changed the verb into a noun describing someone who does the action
Added 'the' (ال) at the beginning, which caused the first letter to be doubled
Added the plural ending ون to show multiple wrongdoers. This word describes هم (they) in this ayah.
This is a basic emphasis particle that adds certainty to what follows
Adding ما changes the meaning from simple emphasis to restriction/limitation
Added vowels to make the basic past tense form

steps.m: he was (describes the state of قَوْلَ 'saying')
steps.n: The middle letter و changes to ا because it's between two vowels. This is a common change in Arabic that makes the word easier to say.
Changed the verb 'to speak' into a noun meaning 'speech' or 'saying'
Added fatha at the end to show this word is the object of كَانَ (was) in the phrase 'was the response'
Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Changed to show who does the action by adding مُ at start and changing middle vowels
Added ين to show there are many believers
Added ال to point to specific believers
Added َ to show these believers are being talked about in the sentence
This is the basic form of the word that refers to a specific point in time

steps.m: when (they are called to Allah and His Messenger)
steps.n: Adding ا at the end changes it from a specific time to a conditional time. In this ayah, it introduces what happens when believers are called to Allah and His Messenger.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels. The final و changes to ا because it's a weak letter at the end.
Changed vowels to make it passive. The weak letter changes from ا to ي in passive form.
Added plural ending وا to show multiple people. The ي changes to و because of the plural ending. The small marks (۟ٓ) are pronunciation helpers.
This is a basic preposition that shows direction or destination. In this ayah, it connects to اللَّهِ (Allah) to show the direction/destination of being called.
Added shadda to emphasize the uniqueness and greatness of Allah

steps.m: of Allah (connected to رَسُولِهِ - His messenger)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection, and changed alif to wasl-alif since it comes after another word
Changes the verb 'to send' into a noun meaning 'one who delivers messages'. The و after second letter makes it intensive, showing this is their main role.
Added و at beginning to connect this word to what came before it (Allah)
Added kasra (ِ) at the end to show this messenger belongs to someone

steps.m: and His messenger (referring to Allah's messenger)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this is specifically Allah's messenger. The small ۦ after ه is just for pronunciation.
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changes to subjunctive mood, often used after 'that' or 'to'

steps.m: so that he may judge (purpose of دُعُوا/they were called)
steps.n: Added لِ at beginning to show this is the purpose or reason for the action in the main clause (they were called)
Changed the root letters into a preposition meaning 'between' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: between them (the believers who were called)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show this means 'between them', referring back to the believers mentioned earlier in the verse
This is a connecting particle that introduces what the believers say. It links to the verb يَقُولُوا۟ (they say) that comes after it.
Added vowels to connect letters. The middle و changes to ا in past tense.
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels. The middle ا returns to و.

steps.m: they say (in response to being called to Allah and His Messenger)
steps.n: Added plural ending to show multiple people. This word appears in the ayah as part of what the believers say when called to Allah and His Messenger.

steps.m: to hear or listen to something

steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense verb showing one male person did the action

steps.m: we heard/listened (part of what believers say in response to being called)
steps.n: Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action. This word appears in what the believers say when they're called to Allah and His Messenger.
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb. The و changes to ا because it's a weak letter.
Added أ at the start to make it mean 'he obeyed' (like adding 'did' in English)
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous word سَمِعْنَا (we heard)
Added نَا at the end to show 'we' did the action, and the long ا vowel is dropped before نَا

steps.m: those (pointing to the believers mentioned before)
steps.n: This is a demonstrative pronoun used to point to people or things that are far away. Here it refers back to the believers who say 'we hear and we obey'.

steps.m: and those (pointing to the believers mentioned before)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
This is the basic form of the pronoun meaning 'they' (for a group of males or mixed gender)
Added damma marks to show this is the subject form of 'they'. This pronoun is connected to الْمُفْلِحُونَ (the successful ones) to say 'they are the successful ones'
Added مُ at start and ِ in middle to show who does the action
Added ال to point to specific successful people and ُ to show this describes 'they' (هُمُ) in the verse
Added ون to show we're talking about multiple people. This word describes 'they' (هُمُ) in أُو۟لٰٓئِكَ هُمُ ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ (those are the successful ones)
This is a special word used to refer to people in a general way, like saying 'whoever' or 'anyone who'. Here it's the subject (doer) of the verb يُطِعِ (obeys).
The و at the beginning means 'and'. It connects this phrase to what came before it in the ayah.
Added vowel marks (fathas) to the root letters to make it mean 'he obeyed' in the past
Added يُ at start to make it 'he' in present tense, middle letter و changed to ي which is common for this type of verb
Added kasra at end to show this is in jussive mood (like after لَمْ)
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for the One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being obeyed in يُطِعِ)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to show emphasis, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb 'obeys' (يُطِعِ)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Changes from a verb (sent) to a noun (messenger) by adding و

steps.m: His messenger (the one being obeyed in يُطِعِ)
steps.n: Added هُ at the end to show it belongs to Him (Allah). This word is being acted upon by the verb يُطِعِ (obeys) in the ayah.

steps.m: and His messenger (the one being obeyed in يُطِعِ)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with what came before it (Allah), showing both are being obeyed

steps.m: to be in awe/fear of something

steps.n: Added vowels to make past tense feeling verb
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at start. The ى shows it's happening now (like 'fears' vs 'feared')

steps.m: and he fears/is in awe (of اللَّهَ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to mean 'and'. The final ى is dropped because this is in a special grammar position (jussive mood) after وَمَن in the ayah
Changed to the proper name Allah by adding ال and merging letters. This is a unique word that became a proper name for the One True God.

steps.m: Allah (as the object being obeyed in يُطِعِ)
steps.n: Added shadda on ل to show emphasis, changed initial alif to wasl form, and added fatha at end to show this is the object of the verb 'obeys' (يُطِعِ)

steps.m: to guard against, be mindful of

steps.n: Form 8 verb (اتّقى) - added ا and ت to show doing the action for protection/benefit of oneself
Added present tense prefix يَ and merged ت with shadda. This verb is connected to يَخْشَ (fears) in the ayah.
Added conjunction وَ meaning 'and' to connect with previous phrase

steps.m: and he is mindful of Him (Allah)
steps.n: Added pronoun هِ meaning 'Him' referring to Allah. The final ي is dropped and replaced with sukun due to jussive mood.
This is a demonstrative pronoun meaning 'those' for pointing to things that are far away - it is a fixed form that doesn't derive from a root
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before - in this case, it's saying 'then/so those' are the ones who obey Allah and His messenger
Added diacritical marks to help with pronunciation: ُ on the hamza, ۟ above و to show it's silent, ٰ to extend the alif, and ٓ to show proper recitation pause
This is the basic form of the third person masculine plural pronoun meaning 'they'
Added damma (ُ) on ه to show this is a subject pronoun
Added damma (ُ) at the end because this pronoun is followed by a word starting with ال
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a past tense verb
Changed to show who does the action. This describes هم (they) in the sentence.
Made definite by adding ال
Added masculine sound plural suffix ون

steps.m: to swear, to take an oath

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to create the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start makes the swearing more intense/serious
Adding وا changes the verb from singular to plural, showing multiple people did the action
Adding وَ connects this word to what came before it
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for deity
Added 'L' sound (shadda) to show this is not just any god, but the one true God - Allah
Added fatha on the shadda for pronunciation
Added kasra at the end to show possession or connection

steps.m: by Allah (object of وَأَقْسَمُوا۟ - they swore)
steps.n: Added بِ at beginning to show doing something by means of Allah. This word is what they swore by in the phrase 'they swore by Allah'.
This is the basic concept of striving or putting in effort
Root meaning 'strive' becomes the noun 'effort' by adding marks (fatha-sukun pattern)
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of their oath (they swore by Allah with their utmost effort)
Added vowels to the root letters to make the noun meaning 'oath' or 'right hand'
Changed to broken plural form to show multiple oaths
Changed ending to show these oaths belong to someone (in this case, 'them')
Added the suffix هِمْ to show these oaths belong to them
This is the basic conditional particle that introduces a condition

steps.m: if indeed, surely if (introducing a condition in أَمَرْتَهُمْ - if you command them)
steps.n: Added لَ at the beginning to add emphasis, like saying 'surely' or 'indeed' in English. This makes the condition stronger.
Added short vowels (fat-ha marks) to make it mean 'he commanded' in the past
Added تَ to show 'you' (masculine singular) did the action in the past. The vowel before ت changes to sukun.
Added هُمْ to show who was commanded (them/they)
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added لَ at beginning to show emphasis and certainty

steps.m: he will surely exit (as an emphatic oath)
steps.n: Added نَّ at the end to make the oath even stronger
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense form

Changed to a command form by: 1) Adding damma on first letter, 2) Dropping the middle weak letter و, and 3) Dropping the final vowel. This word is connected to يَخْرُجُنَّ (they will surely go forth) in this ayah, as Allah is commanding the Prophet to say something about their claims.
This is the basic negative particle used to say 'no' or 'not'
The shadda (ّ) adds emphasis to the negation. In this ayah, it's telling people not to make oaths, connecting to the previous mention of oaths (أَقْسَمُوا)

steps.m: to swear an oath, to divide

steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added تُ prefix to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Changed from present tense to command form and added وا at the end to show it's addressing multiple people
Connected the root letters with vowels. The و changes to ا because it's between two vowels.
Changes from 'he obeyed' (verb) to 'an act of obedience' (noun) by adding ة at the end

steps.m: proper obedience (as a response to لَيَخْرُجُنَّ - they will surely go forth)
steps.n: Adds marks to show this is 'a/an' proper obedience (relates to 'they will go forth')

steps.m: to know or recognize something by its signs/marks

steps.n: Added مَ at start and changed middle letter to و to make it mean 'something known/familiar' rather than 'to know'
Added ة to match with طَاعَةٌ (obedience) which is feminine
Added tanween (double damma) to make the word indefinite

This is a special word called a particle that adds emphasis to what comes after it. The shadda (ّ) makes the ن sound stronger to show extra emphasis, like saying 'definitely' or 'truly' instead of just 'yes'.
Changed the root into the basic noun form meaning 'a deity'
Added fatha mark to show this word is in the accusative case
Connected the root letters with basic past tense voweling
Forms intensive active participle on pattern فَعِيل showing someone with deep knowledge/awareness

steps.m: All-Aware (as a divine attribute of Allah)
steps.n: The tanwin (ٌۢ) shows this is indefinite. When used as Allah's attribute, it indicates complete and perfect awareness of all things.
The basic preposition بِ means 'with' or 'by'. In this ayah, it's used with ما to connect to تَعْمَلُونَ (you do/work).

steps.m: with what (connects to تَعْمَلُونَ - you do/work)
steps.n: Adding ما makes it mean 'with what'. This combination is used to connect 'with' to the verb تَعْمَلُونَ, meaning 'with what you do/work'.
Connected the root letters with fatha to create the basic past tense verb
Changed from past tense 'he worked' to present tense 'you work' by adding تَ at the start

steps.m: you (plural) act/work (in response to Allah's commands)
steps.n: The plural form here refers to all those being addressed in the ayah about their actions in response to Allah's commands
Changed the root into a command by adding a 'oo' sound (damma) and making the middle letter silent (sukun)
The weak letter و drops out between the damma and sukun because it's hard to pronounce
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Changed to a command form by adding وا at the end to tell a group of people to obey. The middle letter changed from و to ي.
Connected the root letters and added vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'deity'
Added a long vowel mark (small alif) after the second letter to extend the sound
Added 'the' to make it refer to a specific deity
Added a fatha mark to show this word is affected by something before it
Added vowels to make past tense verb meaning 'he obeyed'
Added ُ at end to mark present tense indicative mood
Changed from 'I obey' to a command for many people by adding وا at the end
Added connecting particle at beginning
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Changes from verb 'to send' into noun 'messenger' by adding و
Added ال (the) at the start to make it 'the messenger' instead of just 'messenger'
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object of the verb أَطِيعُوا (obey)
This is a conditional particle that introduces a condition. Here it connects to the verb تَوَلَّوْا۟ (they turn away) to mean 'if they turn away'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a response or result to what came before. It's like saying 'then' or 'so' in English
Connected the root letters with basic past tense vowels
Doubling the middle letter shows the action is done with intensity
Adding تَ shows the person is doing the action to themselves
Added وا to show multiple people did the action
Basic emphatic particle that adds certainty to what follows
Added فَ at the beginning to show this follows from what came before
Added ما at the end which restricts or specifies the meaning, making it more focused
This is the basic preposition that shows something is on top of or upon something else
The ى changes to يْ to prepare for connecting with the pronoun. This is just a spelling change that doesn't affect the meaning.

steps.m: upon him (referring to what was assigned to the Messenger)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هِ (him) to show who is being referred to. In this ayah, it refers back to what was assigned to the Messenger.

steps.m: what (connects to حُمِّلَ - was placed as duty)
steps.n: This is a special connecting word that links two parts of a sentence. Here it connects to حُمِّلَ (was placed as duty) to show 'what was placed as duty.'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past tense verb

steps.m: was made to carry (what was assigned to him)
steps.n: Changed to passive form with emphasis. The shadda shows intensity - this wasn't just carried but was specifically assigned or entrusted with something.
Starting with the basic preposition meaning 'upon'
Changed the ending ى to يْ to prepare for adding the next part
Added كُم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Added وَ at the beginning to connect this phrase to what came before it
Added fatha to show the basic pronunciation of the word
Added shadda to make the م sound stronger, which adds emphasis to the word. This word is connected to حُمِّلْتُمْ (you were burdened) in this ayah, asking about what burden was placed.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form basic past tense verb
Changed vowels and added shadda to show something was done to the subject rather than the subject doing it
Changed final vowel to sukun to prepare for suffix addition
Added تُمْ at the end to show this happened to multiple people being spoken to
This is a basic conditional particle that introduces a condition, like saying 'if you do this, then that will happen'

steps.m: and if (connects to تُطِيعُوهُ - you obey him)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this condition with what came before. This introduces a new condition: 'and if you obey him'
Connected the root letters with basic past tense pattern
Changed to present tense with 'you' as the doer
Added و and ا to make it plural 'you all'
Added هُ to show who is being obeyed (referring to the Messenger mentioned in the ayah)
Added vowels to make it past tense 'he guided'
Added اِفْتَعَلَ pattern to show getting guidance for oneself, like adding 'self' to the meaning
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing initial ا with ت to show you (singular) as the doer
Changed ending to show 'you all' instead of 'you' - makes it plural
This is a special word (particle) that doesn't come from a root. It shows something unknown or refers back to something mentioned.

steps.m: and what/that which (that was placed upon the Messenger)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this word to what came before it. In this ayah, it's referring back to what was placed as a duty upon the Messenger.

steps.m: upon/on (preposition showing what is required of ٱلرَّسُولِ)
steps.n: This is a preposition that shows something is required of or placed upon someone/something. In this ayah, it shows what is required of the Messenger.
This is the basic concept of sending or dispatching something
The و after س changes it from 'sending' to 'one who sends/delivers messages a lot'
Adding ال makes it refer to a specific messenger (Muhammad ﷺ in this context). The ر gets doubled (shadda) because it combines with the ل from ال.
The kasra at the end shows this word is being acted upon by the command 'obey' (أَطِيعُوا) in the ayah

steps.m: except/only (limiting what comes before to what comes after)
steps.n: This is a basic word that limits or makes exceptions. It connects 'the messenger' with 'clear delivery' in this ayah, showing that clear delivery is the messenger's only duty.
Changed from action (to deliver) into thing (message/delivery) by adding alif in middle
Added 'the' at beginning and nominative case ending. This word is what comes after 'except' (إِلَّا) in the ayah.
Connected the root letters with fatha to form the basic past tense verb
The ال makes it definite (the), and the ُ shows it's describing the word ٱلْبَلٰغُ (the message/conveying) mentioned earlier in the ayah

steps.m: he promised (subject of promise made by اللَّهُ)
steps.n: Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb. In this ayah, Allah (اللَّهُ) is making the promise.
Added ال to specify this is THE one true God, and the hamza merged with the lam creating a doubled letter
Added the shadda to show the doubled lam, changed alif to wasl-alif since it connects to previous word, and added damma to show this is the subject doing the promising in this verse
Added ين to make it refer to multiple people
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific. The ل combines with ذ to make a stronger ذ sound (shown by ّ). Added َ at the end because it's being used as the subject of ءَامَنُوا۟ (they believed).
Added alif after first letter to strengthen meaning from just trusting to actively believing
Added وا at the end to show multiple people did the action. This word is one of the subjects that Allah promised in this verse.

steps.m: from/among (preposition connecting ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ to what follows)
steps.n: This is a preposition showing which people from the believers Allah is addressing

steps.m: from/among you all (referring to the believers)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to specify who is being referred to - in this case, the believers who were just mentioned
Connected the root letters with vowels to make past tense verb
Added وا to show multiple people did the action (they)

steps.m: and they did righteous deeds (connected to الصَّالِحٰتِ)
steps.n: Added و to connect this word with ءَامَنُوا (believed) before it, showing these people both believed AND did good deeds

steps.m: to make right, to be good

steps.n: Basic past tense verb meaning 'he' did the action - changed from root letters to a complete word
Makes the word refer to multiple feminine subjects
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Shows this word is the object of a preposition
Added basic vowels to connect the root letters
Adds meaning of actively seeking/requesting succession
Changes from past to present/future tense

steps.m: he will surely appoint as successor
steps.n: Adds emphasis - means 'definitely/surely will'

steps.m: he will surely appoint as successor
steps.n: Adds لَ to show strong emphasis, like saying 'surely/definitely'

steps.m: he will most definitely appoint as successor
steps.n: Adds نَّ for extra emphasis, making the promise even stronger

steps.m: he will most definitely appoint them as successors
steps.n: Adds هُمْ to show the promise applies to multiple people
This is a basic word that shows location or position. It connects the word before it to ٱلْأَرْضِ (the earth) that comes after it, showing where something happens.
The ي at the end changes to ى when it's at the end of a word. This doesn't change the meaning - it's just a writing rule.
Added vowel marks to make the basic noun. This word comes after فِى (in/on) in this ayah.
Adding definite article to make the noun definite
Added kasra at the end because it comes after فِى (in/on). The kasra shows this word is being acted upon by فِى.
Added fatha to show this is the comparison particle كَ that means 'like' or 'similar to'

Added ما to make the comparison more specific and emphatic. This compound particle كَمَا connects what comes before it with what comes after it, showing they are similar. Here it connects how Allah will grant authority to the believers similar to how He granted it to those before them.
This is the basic meaning of the root
Added vowels to make a basic past tense verb
Added است at the beginning to make it mean 'causing someone to follow/succeed' instead of just 'following'
Added ن to make it refer to multiple people instead of just one

steps.m: those who (referring to the believers mentioned in ءَامَنُوا۟)
steps.n: Added ال with shadda to point to specific people - in this case, the believers who were just mentioned in the ayah
This is a basic preposition showing where something starts or comes from. Here it combines with كُمْ to show 'from you all'
This is the basic meaning of the root
Added kasra at the end to show this word is owned by something

steps.m: before them (referring to those who came before the believers mentioned in وَعَدَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا)
steps.n: Added هم which means 'them', referring back to the previous people mentioned
Added shadda (double letter) and vowels to make the past tense verb 'he established firmly'
Adding يُ shows this happens in present/future. The middle letter gets kasra (ِ) because of the shadda.
Adding لَ shows this will definitely happen in the future. Allah is making a promise here.

steps.m: surely he will give strong authority
steps.n: Double ن with shadda (نَّ) makes this an extra strong promise or oath

steps.m: and surely he will give strong authority
steps.n: Adding وَ connects this to Allah's previous promise about making them successors in the land
This is the basic preposition that shows direction or purpose

steps.m: for them/to them (referring to their religion that Allah chose for them)
steps.n: Added the pronoun هُمْ to show who the preposition is directed towards. This connects to دِينَهُمُ (their religion) in the ayah.

steps.m: to follow/practice a way of life

steps.n: The root letters get a long 'ee' sound to make it mean 'the way' instead of 'to follow a way'
Changes from 'religion' to 'was followed as a religion' by adding vowel marks

steps.m: their way of life/religion (object of يُمَكِّنَنَّ)
steps.n: Added هُم at the end to show this belongs to them

steps.m: their way of life/religion (object of يُمَكِّنَنَّ)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to help pronunciation when connecting to next word

This is a conditional particle that introduces a future condition. In this ayah, it introduces what should happen when children reach puberty.

steps.m: and when (connects this condition to previous rules)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this rule with previous instructions in the surah about asking permission.

steps.m: to reach, arrive at, or attain

steps.n: This is the basic past tense form showing someone reached something. In this ayah, it's talking about children reaching puberty, which triggers the requirement to ask permission.
Added vowel marks to form the basic noun meaning 'child'
Changed from singular 'child' to plural 'children' by following the pattern أَفْعَال - a common way to make plurals in Arabic
Added the definite article ال to specify these particular children
Added ُ at the end to show this word is the subject of the sentence
Basic preposition meaning 'from' - shows something coming away from or out of something else
Added كم at the end to show this applies to multiple people being talked to
Added ُ at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word (ٱلْحُلُمَ)
Forms the noun meaning 'puberty/maturity'
Makes the word plural by adding damma vowels
Added 'al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the' - makes it specific puberty/maturity
Added fatha at the end to show this is the object being reached in 'when the children reach puberty'
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added است at beginning to show seeking or requesting permission
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end
Added وا to show multiple people doing the action
Added فَلْ at beginning to show 'then let' meaning
This is a comparison particle that shows similarity

steps.m: just as (connects to ٱسْتَـْٔذَنَ - sought permission)
steps.n: Adding مَا makes the comparison more specific, connecting it to the way others sought permission before. This word connects the permission-seeking of the children to how those before them did it.
Added the vowels 'a' and 'i' to make it mean 'he gave permission' in the past
Added است at the beginning to change the meaning from giving permission to seeking it. The hamza moved after the ت
Base form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Added ال with shadda to make it definite and emphasize its connecting role

steps.m: those who (referring to those mentioned before)
steps.n: Added ين to make it refer to multiple people. This connects to the previous mention of children (الْأَطْفٰلُ) who have reached puberty.
Basic preposition showing origin or selection from a group. In this ayah, it's used with كُمُ to mean 'from among you all' when discussing children reaching maturity.
Root letters got vowel marks (fatHa and sukoon) to make the word mean 'before'

steps.m: before them (referring to those who came earlier)
steps.n: Added هِمْ meaning 'them' at the end, with ِ connecting it to the word. This refers back to the people mentioned earlier in the ayah.
Starting with the demonstrative base ذا (this). The alif is written with a special mark (ٰ) in this word.
Adding لِ changes the meaning from 'this' to 'that', making it point to something farther away
Adding كَ at the end makes the word complete. This كَ is different from the 'like' كَ that will be added next.

steps.m: like that, thus (connects to يُبَيِّنُ - He makes clear)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the beginning means 'like' or 'similar to'. In this ayah, it shows how Allah makes things clear in a similar way to what was mentioned before.
Added vowels to connect the root letters
Doubling the middle letter intensifies the meaning
Changed from past to present tense by adding يُ at start. The ُ ending shows this is happening now by Allah
Changed the root into a noun meaning 'deity' or 'god'
Adding 'the' makes it refer to a specific god
Shows this word is the subject of the sentence
Added fatha to the letter lam to show its pronunciation
Added كُمْ at the end to show this is directed to multiple people being addressed
Forms the singular noun for 'sign' from the root. The long آ comes from combining أ and ي.
Adds feminine plural marker to the singular form. This word is the direct object (what is being made clear) of the verb يُبَيِّنُ (yubayyinu - He makes clear).
Adds -i ending to show these signs belong to something/someone
Adds 'his' to show these are Allah's signs. This word is what Allah is making clear in the verse.
Added ُ (damma) at the end to show this is the doer of the action (showing signs/verses)

steps.m: and Allah (the one who shows signs in this ayah)
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this to the previous statement, and added ٱ (hamzat al-wasl) which helps pronunciation
Connected the root letters with vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters to show this is a quality or characteristic
Added two small dashes (tanween) above the word to show it's describing Allah as All-Knowing in this ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to form the basic past tense verb

Changed the vowels and added ي to show someone who has complete wisdom as their nature. The tanwin (ٌ) at the end shows this is describing Allah directly. This word appears right after اللَّهُ (Allah) in the ayah.

steps.m: to sit, remain seated, stay in one place
one who remains seated/stays in place
steps.n: Changes verb to describe someone who does this action regularly or as a characteristic

steps.m: elderly women who sit/remain at home (doer)
steps.n: The word changes from 'one who sits' to 'elderly women who sit' by adding و to make a special feminine plural form

steps.m: the elderly women who sit/remain at home
steps.n: Adding the definite article ال makes the noun definite

steps.m: and the elderly women who sit/remain at home
steps.n: Adding the conjunction و connects this phrase to the previous statement

steps.m: and the elderly women who remain at home (subject of لَيْسَ)
steps.n: Final adjustments: 1) Changed alif to alif waslah (ٱ), 2) Changed alif to alif with bar (ٰ) for proper writing, 3) Added damma (ُ) at end to show this is the subject of لَيْسَ (is not)
This is the basic form of the preposition that shows something comes from or is part of something else
Added fatha at the end to help connect smoothly to the next word ٱلنِّسَآءِ (the women). This is like adding a connecting sound between words.
Changes from basic root to irregular plural meaning 'women' - one of the most common Arabic words for groups of women
Makes the noun definite and adds emphasis with shadda
Adds kasra at the end to show this word follows مِنَ (from) in the ayah, and lengthens the alif
This is the basic form of the feminine plural relative pronoun, used to point to multiple feminine subjects who do something

steps.m: those who (feminine plural, subject of لَا يَرْجُونَ)
steps.n: Added ٱل with shadda to make it more specific and point to a particular group. The ى at the end is just a spelling variation of ي. This word refers to the older women mentioned before who don't expect marriage.
This is a negative particle that makes the verb يَرْجُونَ (they hope) into 'they do not hope'. It's like adding 'not' before a verb in English.
Added a fatha mark (َ) on the ل to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'laa'
Connected the root letters with fatha marks. The final و changes to ا in past tense.
Added يَ at start to show present tense. The middle letter gets sukun, and the final letter gets damma, changing ا back to و.
Added نَ at the end to show this refers to a group of women doing the action. This matches with النِّسَآءِ (the women) mentioned earlier in the verse.

steps.m: marriage (what they do not hope for)
steps.n: Changes the verb into a noun meaning the act of marriage. This word is what is being hoped for in the phrase 'they do not hope for marriage'.

steps.m: marriage (what they do not hope for)
steps.n: The ًا ending shows this word is receiving the action of 'not hoping for'
Adding vowels to make it a usable past tense verb meaning 'is not'
Added فَ at the beginning to show this is a result or consequence of what came before in the verse
Added vowel marks to the basic preposition على which means 'upon' or 'on'
Changed the ending to prepare for connecting with the pronoun. The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns.
Added هِنَّ at the end to show this means 'upon them' specifically for a group of females

steps.m: to lean away from the right path

steps.n: Changes from verb 'to lean away' into noun meaning 'sin/blame'
Added tanween dammah (ٌ) to show this is the subject of لَيْسَ (there is not) in the phrase لَيْسَ عَلَيْهِنَّ جُنَاحٌ (there is no blame upon them).

steps.m: that (introduces the clause يَضَعْنَ - they lay down)
steps.n: This is a particle that connects two parts of the sentence, showing that what comes after it explains or completes what came before. Here it introduces what the women are allowed to do (laying down their outer garments).

steps.m: to place or put something down

steps.n: Added vowels to connect the root letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from past to present by adding يَ at the start. The ُ at the end shows it's happening now.
Added نَ at the end to show multiple women are doing the action. This word refers to elderly women placing their outer garments.
Changed the root letters into a simple noun meaning 'garment' by adding vowels
Changed to broken plural form for multiple garments

steps.m: their clothes (referring to elderly women mentioned earlier in the verse)
steps.n: Added feminine plural possessive ending to show these are the clothes belonging to the elderly women mentioned in the verse
Changed from basic root letters to a common word meaning 'other than' by adding the vowels َ (fatha) and ْ (sukun)
Added fatha at the end to show this word is describing 'their garments' in the verse
Added ت at start and doubled ر to show someone doing this action to themselves deliberately
Added مُ at start and ِ under ر to describe someone who does this action
Added ات to make it refer to multiple women

steps.m: women who show off/display themselves (describing غَيْرَ)
steps.n: Added ٍ to show this word describes غَيْرَ (meaning 'without' here)
Root meaning 'beautify' becomes a noun meaning 'beauty' by adding -eena pattern
Added 'bi' (بِ) at start to mean 'with', and tanwin (ٍ) at end to make it indefinite (like 'a' instead of 'the')
This is a particle that introduces a new clause or statement. It's often used to connect ideas or show purpose.
Added و at the beginning to connect this with the previous statement. In this ayah, it connects with the idea that older women can remove some outer garments.
Added letters at start to show seeking or trying to achieve chastity
Changed first letter to show this happens in present/future time
Added نَ at the end to show many women are doing this action
Changes from root meaning into noun meaning 'good'
Adding double dammah makes it an indefinite noun
Starting with the basic preposition ل which shows direction or purpose
The ل gets a shadda because it comes after another ل in the previous word, making them merge together for smoother pronunciation
Added هُ to show who the preposition is directed to
Added نَّ to change it from singular masculine (him) to plural feminine (them)
This is the proper name of God in Arabic. The doubled ل (lam) with shadda is a unique feature of this word.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ)
steps.n: The ُ (damma) at the end shows that Allah is the subject of the sentence, doing the actions of hearing and knowing (سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ)

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of سَمِيعٌ عَلِيمٌ)
steps.n: Added وَ (wa) at the beginning to mean 'and', connecting this statement to the previous one. The ٱ (alif with waṣla) is added because Arabic words don't start with a silent letter.

steps.m: one who hears perfectly (attribute of Allah)
steps.n: Added ي between second and third root letters and vowels to create intensive form showing complete/perfect hearing ability
The double damma (ٌ) at the end shows this is a complete standalone description of Allah being All-Hearing
Root letters got vowels to make past tense 'he knew'
Adding ي makes this an intensive form showing complete and perfect knowledge. This form is often used for Allah's attributes.

steps.m: All-Knowing (as one of Allah's attributes mentioned with سَمِيعٌ/All-Hearing)
steps.n: The tanween (double damma) shows this is indefinite. Here it appears with another attribute سَمِيعٌ (All-Hearing) describing Allah.
This is a special verb that only comes in past tense form but gives present meaning. It's used to negate things.
Adding the final vowel mark completes this special negation verb
This is the basic form showing the concept of being high or elevated
Adding ى at the end turns this into a preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'. In this ayah, it shows the relationship between a ruling and the blind person (ٱلْأَعْمَىٰ) being discussed.
This form shows something happened to someone rather than them doing something
Added vowel marks (fatha, kasra, fatha) to make 'he became blind' in past tense
The hamza prefix and alif at the end make it into an adjective describing someone with this quality

steps.m: the blind person (subject of لَيْسَ عَلَى)
steps.n: Adding ال makes it refer to a specific blind person. The word appears after لَيْسَ عَلَى (there is not upon) in this ayah.
This root carries the basic idea of something being tight, restricted, or difficult
Added fathas to connect the letters and make it into a past-tense verb

Changed the ending to tanween damma to make it a noun meaning 'blame' or 'difficulty'. This word is the subject of لَيْسَ (there is not) in this ayah, showing there is no blame/difficulty on certain people.
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it

steps.m: and not (negates حَرَجٌ 'difficulty' for ٱلْأَعْرَجِ 'the lame')
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it, showing another category of people who have no difficulty

steps.m: upon/against (showing no blame is upon الْأَعْرَجِ - the lame)
steps.n: This is a basic preposition showing something being placed upon or against something else. In this ayah, it's used to say there is no blame placed upon certain groups of people.

steps.m: to walk with difficulty or limp

steps.n: Added vowels to make it a past tense verb
Adding أ at start changes meaning to 'cause someone to do the action'
