Added fathas to connect the letters and make it into a noun that means the state of being restricted
The double-damma (ٌ) at the end shows this is any general difficulty, not a specific one. This word follows لَيْسَ (there is not) to say 'there is no difficulty'.
Basic negative particle that negates the word حَرَجٌ (difficulty) in this ayah
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with the previous one, showing another case where there is no difficulty
Basic preposition showing responsibility or burden in this context. It connects with ٱلْمَرِيضِ (the sick person) to show there is no burden upon them.
Added ي between second and third root letters and vowel marks to show someone who has this quality
Added ال at beginning to make it specific. This word follows عَلَى (upon) in the ayah.

steps.m: upon the sick person (object of عَلَى)
steps.n: Added kasra at the end because it follows the word عَلَى (upon) in the ayah
Added short 'a' vowels (fatha) to the root letters to make a complete word meaning 'difficulty'
Added tanween damma to show this is an indefinite noun that is the subject of لَيْسَ (there is not)
This is a negative particle that negates what comes after it
Added و at the beginning to connect this phrase with what came before it
This is the basic preposition form showing something is on top of or affecting something else
The alif at the end is written with a special mark (ٰ) called an alif khanjariya, which shows it's a long 'aa' sound
The small alif (ٓ) shows this word connects to the next word أَنفُسِكُمْ (yourselves), meaning 'upon yourselves'
Added vowels to make the basic noun meaning 'self' or 'soul'
Changed the word to show we're talking about multiple selves/souls by adding أ at the start and ُ in the middle
Added كُمْ at the end to show these are your (plural) selves - like saying 'yourselves' in English
This is a connecting word that joins two parts of the sentence. Here it introduces what is not blameworthy - eating from these houses. It connects 'there is no blame' with 'you eat'.
The basic meaning of eating or consuming
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the start
Added وا at the end to show multiple people are doing the action

steps.n: Basic preposition showing where something comes from or starts. In this ayah, it connects the houses that people can eat from to the act of eating.

steps.m: from (connects بُيُوت houses to تَأْكُلُوا۟ eat)
steps.n: Added a small ن (called nun ghunna) at the end to help with pronunciation when connecting to the next word. This doesn't change the meaning.
Changed to make it mean 'houses' instead of just one house
Changes to basic noun pattern meaning 'house'
Changes the word to show many houses instead of just one
Adds kasra to show this word connects to what comes after it
Adds كُمْ to show these houses belong to all of you
A connecting word that lists different choices. In this ayah, it's listing different houses you can eat from - your houses OR your parents' houses OR your siblings' houses, etc.
Connected the root letters to form the basic word for a single house
Changed the vowels and added و to make it plural, showing multiple houses
Added kasra at the end to show these houses belong to 'your fathers' (ءَابَآئِكُمْ)

steps.m: to be a father or care for someone like a father
steps.n: Changed to mean multiple fathers
Root letters أ-ب-و joined together to make the noun 'father'
Changed from singular 'father' to plural 'fathers' by adding آء at the end and lengthening the first vowel
Added ئِكُمْ at the end to show 'your' (plural) - the ئِ is needed when adding كُمْ to words ending in ء

steps.m: to stay, to dwell, to house

steps.n: Root letters put together with vowels to make the simple noun 'house'
Changed the word's internal structure to show multiple houses by adding و and changing vowels
Added ِ at the end to show these houses belong to someone (in this case, the fathers mentioned in the verse)
Root letters أ-م-م became the word 'mother' by adding a damma (u sound) and doubling the middle letter م

Changed regular alif to elongated alif (ٰ) which shows the same long 'aa' sound
Added كُمْ to show possession by multiple people being addressed

steps.m: or (connecting options in بُيُوتِكُمْ 'your houses' and بُيُوتِ ءَابَآئِكُمْ 'houses of your fathers')
steps.n: This is a basic connecting word that gives choices between options. In this ayah, it's used to list different houses where it's permitted to eat.

steps.m: or (connecting options in بُيُوتِكُمْ 'your houses' and بُيُوتِ ءَابَآئِكُمْ 'houses of your fathers')
steps.n: Added the vowel mark fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و to show correct pronunciation.
Changed root into a noun meaning a place where people dwell

steps.m: houses of (connected to أُمَّهٰتِكُمْ - your mothers)

steps.m: to be brothers with someone, to treat as brother

steps.n: Root meaning 'brother' becomes a noun by adding alif at start
Adding vowel marks and elongated alif for proper pronunciation
Adding attached pronoun كم meaning 'your (plural masculine)'

steps.m: or (connecting the houses/places you can eat from)
steps.n: This is a basic connecting word that gives choices between different options. In this ayah, it connects different houses where it's allowed to eat from, like saying 'you can eat from your houses OR your parents' houses OR your siblings' houses'

steps.m: to stay, to dwell, to house

Changes the root into the feminine plural form for 'sisters'. This word is part of the phrase 'houses of your sisters' in this ayah.
Adds possession pronoun for 'your' (plural masculine)
This is a basic connecting word that gives choices between options. In this ayah, it connects different houses that are permissible to eat from.

steps.m: to stay overnight, to live in a place

Changes the word to show multiple houses by adding و and changing vowels

steps.m: of houses (possessed by ءَابَآئِكُمْ - your fathers)
steps.n: Adds ِ at the end to show these houses belong to your fathers (ءَابَآئِكُمْ)
The middle letter is doubled to make it stronger
Changes the word from singular 'paternal uncle' to plural 'paternal uncles' by adding أ at the start and ا after the second root letter. This is a common way to make plurals for family members.
Adds kasra (ِ) to show this word belongs to something or someone (in this case, it will belong to 'you all')
Adds second person masculine plural possessive pronoun

steps.m: or (connecting بُيُوت/houses in a list of choices)

Basic root meaning related to dwelling or housing
Changed from the root word for 'house' to mean multiple houses by adding vowel marks and و - this is a common plural form
Added kasra at the end to show these houses belong to someone - in this case, they belong to آباء (fathers)
Root related to being comprehensive or widespread
Adding vowel marks changes it from root to a usable verb

Added kasra to show this word is possessed by something (comes after بيوت meaning 'houses of')

steps.m: your paternal aunts (in بيوت عَمَّاتِكُمْ - houses of your paternal aunts)

This is a basic connecting word that gives a choice between options. In this ayah, it connects different houses that are permissible to eat in.
Added vowel marks to show exact pronunciation: fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و
Changed the root letters into a noun meaning a place where people stay
Changed the word to show many houses by adding و and changing the vowels
Added ِ to show these houses belong to someone or something

steps.m: to care for or protect as a guardian

Added أَ at the start and changed middle vowels to make the plural form - this is a broken plural pattern

Added كُمْ at the end to show these are 'your' maternal uncles

steps.m: or (connecting بُيُوتِ/houses in a list of choices)
steps.n: This is a basic connecting word that gives choices between options. In this ayah, it connects different houses that people can eat from.

steps.m: or (connecting بُيُوتِ/houses in a list of choices)
steps.n: Added fatha (َ) on أ and sukun (ْ) on و to show correct pronunciation

Adds ِ to show these houses belong to someone or are connected to the next word

steps.m: to be a maternal uncle/aunt, to take care of family

steps.n: Changes from singular 'maternal aunt' to plural 'maternal aunts'
Adds kasra to indicate genitive case
Adds كُمْ to show possession by multiple people being addressed. The alifs are written with overbar (خٰلٰ) in the Uthmani script.

steps.m: or (connecting بُيُوت/houses in a list of choices)
steps.n: This word connects equal options in a list. In this ayah, it's used multiple times to list different houses you can eat from - your parents' houses, your siblings' houses, etc.

steps.m: what/that which (connects to مَلَكْتُم - you possess)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that links to what comes after it. Here it means 'what/that which' referring to the houses you possess the keys to.
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changed from 'he' to 'you' by adding تَ at the end
Changed from second person singular to second person plural by replacing تَ with تُم
Added مِ at the beginning to show this is a tool used for the action
Changed to plural form by adding ا after second root letter
Added هُ at the end to show these keys belong to something, and added emphasis with ّ at the beginning

This is a basic connecting word that gives options. In this ayah, it connects different houses that are permissible to eat from.

steps.m: close friend (someone who is always truthful with you)

This is a special verb used for negation in Arabic. It's irregular and only appears in past tense form but gives present meaning.
Adding the fatha (َ) mark shows this is a past tense verb form
Adding the sukun (ْ) mark shows this letter has no vowel sound
Added the final letter with fatha. In this ayah, it negates the word جُنَاحٌ (blame/sin), saying there is no blame.
Basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'. This is a particle, so we start with its basic form rather than a root.

steps.m: upon (ready to connect to a pronoun)
steps.n: The ى changes to ي with sukun to prepare for connecting to a pronoun
Adds كُمْ to show this applies to multiple people being spoken to
Added vowel marks to transform the action into a noun
Added alif to make it into an abstract concept
Added tanween to show this is the subject (doer) in the phrase ليس عليكم جناح (there is no blame upon you)

Changes to show action happening now by multiple people
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense form
Added تَ at start to show 'you' and changed vowels to show present tense
Added وا at the end to show multiple people doing the action
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the basic past tense verb
Added ي between second and third root letters and changed middle vowel to kasra to show the meaning of being together as a group

steps.m: all together (describing how something is done)
steps.n: Added tanween alif at the end to show this describes how something is done

steps.m: or (connecting بُيُوتِ/houses in a list of choices)
steps.n: This is a connecting word that gives choices between things. In this ayah, it connects different houses that are being listed as options for where people can eat.
Adding alif creates a plural noun showing multiple separated things
Adding fatha vowel mark shows this is an active form
Adding fatha after second letter completes the basic word pattern
Adding tanween alif shows this describes how the action happens (adverb)
Adding أَ at start makes it describe being scattered into groups
This is a time word that shows when something happens. Here it connects to the word دَخَلْتُم (you entered) to show when you should do the action.
Added فَ at the beginning to show this happens after something else. It's like saying 'then' or 'so' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make a past tense verb
Added تُ at the end to change from 'he entered' to 'I entered' in the past
Added م to show multiple people (you all) did the action
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a basic noun for a place where people stay
Changed the vowels and added و to show we're talking about multiple houses

steps.m: houses (as the object being entered in دَخَلْتُم)
steps.n: Added ًا to show these are the houses being entered, connecting to the verb دَخَلْتُم (you entered) in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make a basic past-tense verb
Adding shadda and changing middle vowel to kasra makes it mean actively giving peace to others
Adding وا makes it a command to a group of people
Adding فَ shows this comes after something else mentioned before
Basic preposition showing something is on top of, over, or directed towards something else
Added small alif (ٰ) to show the long 'aa' sound is maintained at the end
Added a small vertical line (ٓ) to show this word connects to the next word أَنفُسِكُمْ (yourselves)
The word 'nafs' (self/soul) becomes plural by adding أَ at the start and changing the middle to 'u' sound, making it mean 'selves/souls'
Adding kasra (ِ) at the end shows this word is 'of' something or belongs to something

steps.m: your own selves (referring to multiple people being addressed)
steps.n: Added كُمْ to show this belongs to multiple people being spoken to

Changed to a noun meaning 'greeting' by adding تَ at start, changing first vowel to kasra, and adding ة at end

steps.m: a greeting (object of فَسَلِّمُوا - so give greetings)
steps.n: Added tanween to show this is the object of the command 'give greetings'
Basic preposition meaning 'from'. Used to show where something starts or comes from.
Added shadda to emphasize the م sound because it comes after another م in the previous word (مَلَكْتُم)
Added sukun to show the ن has no vowel sound after it
Added vowels to make the basic preposition meaning 'at' or 'near'
Added kasra at the end to show this word connects to Allah (اللَّهِ) that comes after it, forming the phrase 'from Allah'
Changed the root into a noun form meaning 'deity'
Added ال (the) and shadda to make it specific, plus kasra at the end to show possession. This is the unique name for the one true God in Islam.

steps.m: to increase, to grow, to bless

steps.n: Adding an alif after first root letter makes it about doing something to/with someone else. This word appears in a descriptive phrase but is not directly related to another word through case.
Adding مُ at the beginning changes it from 'to bless' into 'something that is blessed'
The suffix 'ة' makes the word feminine.  It's like adding '-ness' to some English words to make them nouns.
The ً (tanween) at the end shows this is describing something that is blessed - like saying 'blessed' as an adjective
Connected the root letters with vowels to form basic adjective
Doubled the middle letter to show intensity of the quality
Added ة to make it describe a feminine word

steps.m: a very good/pure/pleasant thing (describing تَحِيَّةً - greeting)
steps.n: Added ً to show this describes the greeting (تَحِيَّةً) mentioned in the verse
Starting with the basic demonstrative word 'this'
Adding لِ changes 'this' to 'that', making it refer to something farther away
Adding كَ at the end makes it more specific, like pointing to something

steps.m: like that (referring to the previous rules mentioned)
steps.n: Adding كَ at the beginning changes it to mean 'like that', referring back to what was just mentioned in the previous verses about rules for eating in others' houses
Added vowels and doubled the middle letter to show intensive/causative meaning
Added يُ at beginning to show present tense, changed middle vowel to kasra, and added damma at end to show it's doing the action
Added shadda to emphasize the ل, which is a special feature of this divine name

steps.m: Allah (subject of يُبَيِّنُ - He makes clear)
steps.n: Added ُ to show this is the subject doing the action in يُبَيِّنُ (He makes clear). Also changed initial alif to wasl form since it comes after another word.
Added fatha to show the basic meaning of direction or purpose
Added كُم at the end to show this is directed to multiple people
Added damma at the end to help connect with the next word (الْءَايٰتِ)

steps.m: to show, make clear, or be apparent

steps.n: Root letters changed to make the noun 'sign/miracle' by adding vowels and changing last letter
Added the feminine ending to specify a single instance
Added the feminine plural ending to show multiple signs
Adding 'the' makes it refer to specific signs
Adding kasra shows this word belongs to something
This is a particle that expresses hope or expectation. In this ayah, it introduces the verb تَعْقِلُونَ (you understand/comprehend)
Added pronoun suffix كُمْ to show this hope/expectation applies to multiple people being addressed. This connects to the verb تَعْقِلُونَ (you understand) that follows.

steps.m: to use one's mind to understand
he used his mind to understand
steps.n: Changed from basic root letters to past tense 'he' form by adding the vowels (َ)

steps.m: he uses his mind to understand
steps.n: Changes from 'he understood' (past) to 'he understands' (present) by adding يَ at the start

steps.m: you use your mind to understand
steps.n: Changed from 'he' to 'you' by replacing يَ with تَ at the start

steps.m: you all use your minds to understand
steps.n: Added ونَ to show that many people are doing this action
Adding shadda makes this word stronger, changing it from 'if' to 'indeed/certainly'
Adding ما changes the meaning from 'indeed' to 'only'. This combination is used to limit something, like saying 'nothing but' or 'just' in English
Added vowels to connect the letters and show this happened in the past
Changes the verb into a noun describing someone who does this action
Adds 'al' (ال) at the start to mean 'the'
Makes it plural to show many believers. This word is what إِنَّمَا (indeed/truly) is talking about in this ayah.
Added ن at the end to make it plural, showing we're talking about multiple people or things

steps.m: those who (refers to believers mentioned in ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ)
steps.n: Added ال at beginning to make it specific. The ل gets a shadda because it combines with the following ذ sound (called solar assimilation).
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the basic past tense verb
Added alif after first letter to strengthen the meaning from just trusting to believing
Added وا at the end to show many people did this action
This is the base word for Allah, God's proper name in Arabic. The shadda shows emphasis on the ل.
Adding the kasra (ِ) at the end shows possession or connection, like saying 'of Allah'

steps.m: in/by Allah (object of يُؤْمِنُونَ - they believe)
steps.n: Adding بِ at the beginning shows how or by what means something is done. In this ayah, it shows what they believe in.

steps.m: and to send or deliver messages
steps.n: Added وَ at the beginning to connect this word with the previous one, like saying 'and'
Changed from basic 'send' verb to 'messenger' noun, with -i ending showing it belongs to someone
Added هِ at the end to show it belongs to Him (Allah)
This is a special word that means 'when' for things that are expected to happen. In this ayah, it introduces what believers should do when they are with the Prophet ﷺ in a group meeting.

steps.n: Adding وَ connects this condition to the previous statement about true believers. It shows this is another quality of believers - that when they are with the Prophet ﷺ in a group meeting, they don't leave without permission.

Added وا at the end to show multiple people (they) did the action. Changed the last vowel to ُ before وا as required by Arabic grammar.
This is a basic preposition showing accompaniment or being together with something/someone

steps.m: with him (refers to being with رَسُولِهِۦ - His Messenger)
steps.n: Added هُۥ at the end to show 'him'. This refers back to the Messenger mentioned earlier in the ayah.

Added alif maksura (ىٰ) at the end to show it's a long vowel sound

steps.m: upon/on (with extra emphasis on أَمْرٍ - matter)
steps.n: Added a small alif (ٓ) above to show connection with the next word أَمْرٍ (matter)

steps.m: to command or deal with a matter

Added tanween kasra to show this is any general collective matter, not a specific one. This matches how it's used in the phrase 'على أمرٍ جامعٍ' (concerning a collective matter).

steps.m: to gather or bring things/people together

steps.n: Added ا after ج and ِ after م to show this describes something that gathers or brings together. This word describes أَمْرٍ (matter/affair) in this ayah.

This is a negative particle that changes the meaning of verbs to 'did not' or 'have not'. In this ayah, it negates يَذْهَبُوا۟ (they go) to mean 'they do not go'.
Added emphasis by doubling the ل with a shadda mark. This is a common change when certain words come before لَمْ.
Connected the root letters with fatha to make the past tense verb
Adding يَ at the start and changing vowels shows this is happening now or in the future
Adding وا makes this plural 'they' instead of singular 'he'. The word follows لَّمْ (not) in this ayah, making it mean 'they do not go'
This is a particle that shows a limit or endpoint. Here it connects the previous action (not leaving) with getting permission.
Added fatha on first letter and shadda with fatha on second letter to show how to pronounce it correctly
Added alif maksura at the end to show the long 'aa' sound
Adding vowels to make it a past tense verb
Added است at beginning to show seeking or requesting permission
Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added و to make it plural - 'they' instead of 'he'
Added هُ to show whose permission they seek
This is a basic emphatic word that adds certainty to what follows it
Added kasra under the إ to show how it's pronounced
Added shadda to make the emphasis stronger. In this ayah, it emphasizes that Allah is truly forgiving and merciful.
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that became the relative pronoun
Adding ال makes it specifically refer to someone or something being described
Adding ين makes it refer to multiple people instead of just one
Adding the vowel marks and changing ال to ٱلّ with shadda for proper pronunciation. This word describes 'the believers' (ٱلْمُؤْمِنُونَ) in this ayah.

Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and changing vowels
Added ون to show multiple people are doing the action
Added كَ to show they are seeking permission from 'you'
Added damma to show this is a demonstrative pronoun base
Added alif maksura with a small alif above it to emphasize distance

steps.m: those who (subject of يَسْتَـْٔذِنُونَكَ - they ask your permission)

steps.m: to trust, believe in, feel secure

steps.n: Adding alif at start changes meaning to 'make someone do the action'
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at start and changing vowels
Added plural ending to show multiple people doing the action. This refers to those who ask permission mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is the base word meaning deity or god, though Allah is a unique proper name that transcends this basic meaning
Adding ال and doubling the ل creates the unique proper name Allah, which is not just 'the god' but the specific name of The One God
The shadda shows that the ل is doubled, emphasizing this is a unique proper name

steps.m: in/by/with Allah (object of belief in ءَامَنُوا۟)

steps.n: The و makes it mean someone who does this action a lot

Adding وَ connects this word to Allah's name that came before it, showing they go together

Added وا to show multiple people doing the action

The ٌ shows this is being used as a complete noun/adjective
This is the basic negative particle that means 'not' or 'no'

steps.n: Changed from singular 'you make' to plural 'you all make' and became a command not to do something (with لا)

steps.m: to call, invite, or pray to someone

steps.n: Changed the word from an action into a noun that means the act itself
Added hamza (ء) at the end because the word ends with a long alif

Added 'al' to make it specific and doubled the ر letter
Added kasra at the end to show this messenger belongs to or is connected to something else mentioned in the sentence

steps.m: calling out (like when asking for help)
steps.n: Changed the word to show the action itself instead of doing the action

Added kasra (ِ) to show this word is owned by something
Added كُم at the end to show this means 'some of you (plural)'

steps.m: to split or divide into parts

steps.n: This turns the idea of splitting into a word for one of the split pieces
The ًا (tanween) at the end shows this word is being acted upon - some people are being called/summoned
This is a special word that adds certainty to the verb يَعْلَمُ (knows) that comes after it. It's like saying 'definitely knows' or 'certainly knows' in English.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense form

steps.m: he knows (subject of قَدْ at start of sentence)
steps.n: Changed to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end. The sukun (ْ) on ل helps with pronunciation. This word comes after قَدْ which emphasizes that Allah definitely knows.
This is a unique word transformation that happened historically to create the proper name for God in Islam. The ال makes it specific to the one true deity.

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - He knows)
steps.n: Added the diacritical marks including shadda for emphasis and damma to show this is the subject doing the action of knowing. In this ayah, Allah is the one who knows those who slip away.
This is the basic form of the relative pronoun that connects descriptions to people or things
Adding ٱلَّ makes it more specific, like saying 'the one' instead of just 'one'

steps.m: those who (referring to people who يَتَسَلَّلُونَ/slip away)
steps.n: Adding نَ makes it refer to many people instead of just one person. In this ayah, it refers to the people who try to slip away secretly.

steps.m: to slip away or extract smoothly

Added ت at start and doubled middle letter to show doing action to oneself
Changed from past to present tense by adding يَ at start and ُ at end - shows ongoing sneaking action
Added ون to show multiple people doing the action
This is the basic preposition meaning 'from'
Added the pronoun كُمْ which means 'you all' to show who is being referred to

steps.m: stealthily, secretly (describing how they slip away)
steps.n: Added ًا to show this word describes how they slip away (يَتَسَلَّلُونَ) - they do it stealthily.
Added vowel marks (fatha-kasra-fatha) to make it a past tense verb meaning 'he became cautious'
Added يَ at start to show this happens in present/future, and changed vowels
Added لِ to make it a command and changed the ending to sukoon

steps.m: then let him be cautious (in response to يَتَسَلَّلُونَ - they slip away)
steps.n: Added فَ to show this is a result of the previous action (them slipping away), and changed ending to kasra because it's connected to what follows
Starting with the basic demonstrative word that points to something
Adding ال and ي changes it from pointing at something to describing who or what did something

steps.m: those who/which (describing يَتَسَلَّلُونَ - those who slip away)
steps.n: Adding ين makes it refer to many people instead of just one. In this ayah, it's describing those people who slip away secretly.

steps.m: those who/which (describing يَتَسَلَّلُونَ - those who slip away)
steps.n: Adding a strong L sound (shadda) and short vowel marks to show the correct Quranic pronunciation, but meaning stays the same
This is the basic meaning of the root letters
Form III shows interaction between two parties - here meaning to actively oppose someone else
Adding يُ at the start and changing vowels shows this happens in the present/future
Adding ون shows multiple people (3 or more males) are doing the action
This is a preposition that shows separation or moving away from something. In this ayah, it shows people moving away from or opposing the command.
This pattern makes the root into a noun meaning 'a single instance or occurrence'
Changed from basic root letters to a noun meaning 'command' by adding vowel marks
Added kasra at the end to show this word is owned by or connected to something else
Added هِ with elongation mark (ۦٓ) to show this command belongs to him. This word follows the preposition عَنْ (about/concerning) in the ayah.
This is a connecting word that joins two ideas together. Here it connects 'they should beware' with what they should beware of - 'that a trial afflicts them'.
Added the fatha mark (َ) above the first letter to show how to pronounce it correctly as 'anna'
This root carries the basic meaning of something striking, hitting, or befalling something else
Changed root letters into past tense verb by adding the vowel 'aa' between first two letters
Changed from past to present tense by adding تُ at start. The middle letter changed from ا to ي to show ongoing action
Changed the ending vowel to show this is dependent on something else

steps.m: that it strikes them (referring to فِتْنَةٌ or عَذَابٌ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ at the end to show who is being struck. This refers back to those who disobey the Prophet's command
Added kasra under first letter and sukun on middle letter to transform verb into noun
Added ة with fatha to make it refer to one specific instance of testing

steps.m: a trial/affliction (subject of تُصِيبَهُمْ - hits/afflicts them)
steps.n: Added tanween dammah to show this is the subject doing the action in تُصِيبَهُمْ (hits/afflicts them)

steps.m: or (connecting تُصِيبَهُمْ فِتْنَةٌ with عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ)
steps.n: This is a basic word that connects two possibilities. In this ayah, it connects two things that could happen to those who disobey: either they could face a trial (فِتْنَةٌ) or they could face a painful punishment (عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ).
Added basic past tense vowels to connect the root letters
Added يُ at start to make it present tense. The middle letter و changed to ي because it's between two vowels (kasra and damma).
Changed the ending from ُ to َ to show it's in the subjunctive mood, which often happens after certain words

steps.m: that it afflicts them (referring to فِتْنَةٌ or عَذَابٌ)
steps.n: Added هُمْ to show who is being affected. The word relates to either فِتْنَةٌ (trial) or عَذَابٌ (punishment) that might afflict them.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to create the basic past tense verb
Adding an alif between the second and third root letters turns this into a noun describing the result of the action. This word is what will strike/afflict them (يُصِيبَهُمْ) in the ayah.
The double damma (tanween) shows this is indefinite - meaning any punishment. This word is what will strike/afflict them (يُصِيبَهُمْ) in the ayah.
The ي between the second and third root letters makes this describe something that causes intense pain
The double dammah (ٌ) makes this word indefinite, meaning 'a' painful thing rather than 'the' painful thing
This is the base negative particle لا meaning 'no'
Adding أَ at the beginning changes this from a negative particle to an attention-getting particle that tells the listener something important is coming
The alif at the end is elongated to emphasize the word and make it more attention-grabbing
This is the basic form of the emphasis particle
Added kasra vowel mark under the alif to show how to pronounce it
Added shadda to strengthen the ن, which makes the word more emphatic. This particle introduces and emphasizes the important statement that follows about everything belonging to Allah.
Added لِ at the beginning to show ownership/belonging and ِ at the end to connect it with what follows. This word shows that everything in the heavens and earth belongs to Allah.
Basic particle that shows what belongs to or relates to something. Here it connects with 'to Allah' (لِلَّهِ) to show what belongs to Him.
Basic particle showing location or position

steps.m: in/within (location in ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ - the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added ى at the end to complete the particle. In this ayah, it shows what belongs to Allah - everything in the heavens and earth.
The root letters form the base meaning of height
Added vowels to connect the letters and make a past tense verb
Changes the verb into a noun meaning the high/elevated place
Makes the word plural to show multiple heavens
Makes the word definite by adding 'the'
Changed from 'the heavens' to 'of the heavens' by adding kasra at the end, showing it belongs to Allah (لله)

steps.m: of the heavens/skies (specifically those belonging to Allah)
steps.n: Adds ال to specify these are the heavens belonging to Allah
The root letters form the base meaning of vastness
Connected the letters with vowel marks to make it a usable word
Added ال at the beginning to make it specific
Added kasra at the end to show this word follows 'in' (فِى) from earlier in the verse

steps.m: and of the earth/land (connected to ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this word with 'heavens' (ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ) mentioned before it
This is a special word that comes before verbs to emphasize them. Here it emphasizes that Allah definitely knows (يَعْلَمُ) what state you are in.
Connected the root letters with fatha marks to make the past tense verb
Changed from past tense (he knew) to present tense (he knows) by adding يَ at the start and changing the vowels
This is the basic form of the word that can mean either 'what' (as a question) or 'that which' (referring to something). In this ayah, it means 'that which' and refers to what the people are upon.
Added fatha to show correct pronunciation

steps.m: what/that which (referring to what you are upon)
steps.n: The alif is elongated (آ) to show it should be pronounced longer. In this ayah, it refers to the state or condition that the people are upon.
This is the basic form of the word used to talk directly to multiple people

steps.m: you all (when talking to a group)
steps.n: Adding م at the end makes it clear we're talking to multiple people
This is the basic preposition meaning 'upon' or 'on'
The ى changes to يْ when connecting to pronouns

steps.m: upon him/on him (referring back to what is in the heavens and earth)
steps.n: Added هِ which means 'him', referring back to what belongs to Allah in the heavens and earth mentioned earlier in the verse
Connected the root letters with fatha on first letter and sukoon on middle letter to make the basic noun form
Added fatha at the end to show this is the time when they will be returned (يُرْجَعُونَ)

steps.m: and (on) the day (when they will be returned)
steps.n: Added و at beginning to connect this to what came before
Basic past tense verb meaning 'he returned'. Made by adding the vowels (fat-ha) to the root letters.
The يُ prefix shows someone/something is being returned rather than returning themselves

steps.m: they are being returned (referring to those who deny Allah's knowledge)
steps.n: Changes from singular 'he' to plural 'they'. This refers back to those who deny Allah's knowledge of what they are doing, as mentioned earlier in the ayah.
This is a preposition that shows direction or destination
The ى changes to ي with sukun to connect with the next part

steps.m: to him, towards him (referring back to اللَّهِ)
steps.n: Added هِ at the end to show this means 'to him', referring back to Allah mentioned earlier in the ayah
Connected the root letters with fatha vowels to make the basic past tense verb
Added emphasis by doubling the middle letter to show the news is being told with importance or intensity
Changed to present tense by adding يُ at the start and changing middle vowel to kasra (ِ). The hamza (ئ) appears because of spelling rules.

steps.m: and then he tells important news intensively
steps.n: Added the conjunction فَ (and then) at the beginning of the word

steps.m: and then he will inform them (about عَمِلُوا۟ what they did)
steps.n: Added هُم (them) at the end to show who receives the news. This word connects to عَمِلُوا۟ (what they did) in the ayah.
This is a relative pronoun meaning 'what' - it refers to the deeds/actions mentioned after it in عَمِلُوا (they did)
Added the preposition بِ at the beginning to show this connects to the verb يُنَبِّئُهُم (inform them) - it shows what they will be informed about
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb

steps.m: they did/worked (subject of يُنَبِّئُهُم - He will inform them)
steps.n: Added وا with ُ to show multiple people did the action. This word is what they will be informed about in يُنَبِّئُهُم (He will inform them).
This is a unique proper name that doesn't come from a root. It's the personal name of God in Islam.
Added shadda on ل to show it's pronounced strongly

steps.m: Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - knows)
steps.n: Added damma at the end to show this is the doer of the action (knows) in this sentence

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - knows)
steps.n: Added و at the beginning to connect this part to what came before it in the verse

steps.m: And Allah (as the subject of يَعْلَمُ - knows)
steps.n: Changed the alif to a special form (ٱ) used in the Quran for the definite article
Added damma and shadda to make a word meaning 'all' or 'every'
Added kasra to show this connects to the word شَىْءٍ (thing) that comes after it, making the phrase 'of every thing'
Added بِ at the beginning to show Allah is 'with/by' every thing in His knowledge, connecting to عَلِيمٌۢ (All-Knowing)
Added fatha on first letter and sukun on middle letter to form the basic noun. The hamza at the end is part of the word's basic structure.
Added tanwin kasra at the end to show this thing is possessed by Allah, as mentioned in the ayah (لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمٰوٰتِ - to Allah belongs what is in the heavens)
Connected the root letters with vowels to make a past tense verb
Changed to intensive form showing complete knowledge. This word describes Allah in this ayah.
Added two small marks at the end to show this describes Allah as the doer of the action
